For Loner, the following figure shows the PDSCH-DMRS transmission processing chain.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating the process of a Loner FPGA Transmitter (Tx). The flowchart is divided into two main sections: "Tx Encoder" and "Tx Front End".
The "Tx Encoder" section details the steps involved in preparing the data for transmission, starting with a "Transport Block (TB): Payload" received from L2-LO. This is followed by "TB CRC attachment", "Code Block (CB) Segmentation & CB CRC Attachment", "LDPC encoding", "Rate matching", "Code block concatenation", and "Scrambling". Each step shows the input and output data formats, such as bits and codewords.
The "Tx Front End" section describes the processing of the scrambled data, including "Modulation", "Layer mapping", "Precoding", "RE mapping & per channel power adjustment". Within this section, a dashed red box highlights "L1Low" which encompasses "Phase Compensation", "iFFT", and "CP and GP addition".
Finally, the flowchart shows an output interface labeled "CPRI/eCPRI" leading to a "DFE" block. A note at the bottom indicates that when feature 5GC000399 is used, the L1 FPGA-DFE is an eCPRI interface and L1Low is implemented in RU (DFE).
The flowchart uses a standard top-down approach with rounded rectangles representing processing steps and arrows indicating the flow of data. The text within the flowchart describes the operations and data transformations at each stage.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3904.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The Loner, the one Intel Stratix-10 FPGA, is used for all L1 processing. The Loner is composed of two distinct portions:

* A Hard Processor System (HPS) portion which consists mainly of a Quad-Core ARM Cortex-A53 MPCore processor. The L1 Software (L1-SW) is deployed on the HPS.
* An FPGA portion which consists mainly of an FPGA fabric known also as FPGA Programmable Logic (PL) which performs all the DL and UL L1 Base Band processing. This portion is referred to as L1-FPGA.

For Loki, the following figure shows the PDSCH-DMRS transmission processing chain.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating the process of PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) transmission in a mobile communication system. The diagram depicts interactions between different components labeled "MHABS@Loki", "MDABS@Loki", "ARM Cores@Loki", and "L2\_SW@SnowFish". These components are further broken down into sub-components like "CPRI", "DLFE", "RFOE", "eCMP", "LENC", "L1\_SW\_DSP", "L1\_SW\_ARM", "L2\_PS", and "L2\_Lo".
The diagram is structured with a "loop" indicating that the process occurs "every slot when PDSCH-DMRS-PTRS Transmission should occur".
A note at the top clarifies that the figure shows logical steps and parameters, not exact timing or implementation control flow.
The main flow of the diagram can be summarized as follows:
1. **gNB eCPRI Startup**: This phase involves eCPRI startup with RP1, delay measurement, configuration, cell mapping, cell configuration, and cell setup.
2. **Feed PDSCH related jobs to PSM**: This step involves feeding PDSCH-related jobs to the PSM (Packet Switched Module) for a specific slot #j. The configuration uses an instance of the LENC\_TASK\_CFG\_S structure.
3. **Transport Block (TB) Payload Receiving from L2 and build Job Configuration**: This is a detailed section describing the inputs and configurations for building the job.
\* **Inputs from L2**: The payload from L2 is 64-bit aligned. The inputs include `DIData_PdschPayloadTbSendReq`, `tbIndex`, `tbFragmentSize_bytes`, `payload`, `pdschEncoderConfig`, and `tbSize_bits`.
\* **Configuration for LENC**:
\* Input configuration for LENC specifies `DATA_BIT_MSB_FIRST` and `DATA_BYTE_ORDER_MODE_0` for the L2 payload format.
\* Output configuration for LENC involves one segment per OFDM symbol with data packing to resource elements enabled. It includes parameters like `BYPASS SEGMENTATION`, `OUTPUT_DATA_ORDER` (set to `DATA_BIT_LSB_FIRST`, `DATA_BYTE_ORDER_MODE_0`), `NUM_TOTAL_SYM_PER_LAYER` (calculated by L1-SW), and `NUM_LAYER`.
\* **Outputs of LENC**: LENC uses a 128-bit single-threaded write DMA port. The outputs are 128-bit aligned, with 1 RE taking 1 byte (up to 256QAM, 1024QAM not implemented), and `num_layers * num_total_sym_per_layer` bytes stored per OFDM symbol.
4. **Message Sequence**: Several messages are exchanged between components:
\* `DIData_SlotTypeReq`
\* `DIData_PdschSendReq(0)`
\* `DIData_PdschPayloadTbSendReq()`
\* `DIData_PdschPayloadTbSendReq() if fragmented`
5. **Job Creation**:
\* A job is created for LENC for PDSCH transmission.
\* MDAB and eCMP/RFOE jobs are created immediately without waiting for LENC and are set to run on a timer via `WAIT_CMD`.
\* A job is created for MDAB for PDSCH transmission.
The diagram uses dashed lines to represent the lifelines of the components and arrows to indicate the flow of messages and data. Yellow boxes are used to group related steps and provide detailed information.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3906.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating the process of PDSCH transmission. The diagram depicts interactions between different components[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3906.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating a communication flow between different components. The diagram is divided into two main sections: an "alt" (alternative) block and a "CPRI" block.
The "alt" block contains two conditions: "[if eCPRI]" and "[if CPRI]". Under the "[if eCPRI]" condition, there is a message labeled "jobDone" sent from an unspecified component to another, followed by a message labeled "DIData\_PdschSendReq -eCpriConfig" sent from the second component back to the first.
Under the "[if CPRI]" condition, there are three "jobDone" messages. The first "jobDone" message is sent from an unspecified component to another. The second "jobDone" message is sent from the second component to a third. The third "jobDone" message is sent from the third component to a fourth.
Below the sequence diagram, there are several rectangular boxes representing different components or modules. These are labeled as: "CPRI", "DLFE", "RFOE", "eCMP", "LENC", "L1\_SW\_DSP", "L1\_SW\_ARM", "L2\_PS", and "L2\_Lo". The "CPRI" box is associated with the "[if CPRI]" section of the diagram, and the "L1\_SW\_DSP" box is associated with the "[if eCPRI]" section. The other boxes are positioned below the main diagram without explicit connections shown in this view.
The diagram uses dashed vertical lines to represent the lifelines of the components and solid arrows to indicate message exchanges. The overall visual style suggests a technical diagram, likely related to telecommunications or embedded systems.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3906.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

## 4.3.2 Startup configuration

## 4.3.3 Cell configuration

### 4.3.3.1 LTE-CRS mapping patterns

#### 4.3.3.1.0-1 LTE-CRS mapping patterns Refer to ( 11436266 ) for configurations of LTE-CRS mapping patterns which are carried in DlCell_SetupReq message. (ID: 11429422)

### 4.3.3.2 Asymmetric FDD DSS configuration

**4.3.3.2.0-1**  (ID: `12621147`)

Asymmetric FDD DSS configuration
Asymmetric FDD DSS is the configuration with NR FDD carrier being larger than LTE carrier, and the center frequency of LTE and NR is different (see lteDlCenterFrequencyScOffset in [11512156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRnXwPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)).

The supported asymmetric DSS are listed in the following table:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| LTE FDD (MHz) | NR FDD (MHz) |
| 10 (50 PRBs) | 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 50 |
| 15 (75 PRBs) | 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 50 |
| 20 (100 PRBs) | 25, 30, 35, 40, 50 |

The supported technology types are shown in the table below:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Technology | Loki | Thor (BB) |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | [CB007372-A] Supported [End CB007372-A] | [CB007372-A] Supported [End CB007372-A] |
| FR1-FDD-eCRPI-7-2a | [CB007372-J] Supported [End CB007372-J] | [CB007372-J] Supported [End CB007372-J] |

---

## 4.3.4 Dynamic configuration

### 4.3.4.1 PDSCH Message

#### 4.3.4.1.1 PDSCH Interface for non-eCPRI-7.2e split

**4.3.4.1.1.0-2**  (ID: `12713642`)

PDSCH Interface for non-eCPRI-7.2e split
Upon reception of *DlData\_PdschSendReq and* DlData\_PdschPayloadTbSendReq messages (See [11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11513002](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRtebfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G-L1-DL shall generate the PDSCH signals (with associated DMRS and PTRS) and transmit them in the *sfn and* slot as indicated in the both messages.\*\*\*\*

Information needed to generate the PDSCH signal are:

* the RNTI associated with the PDSCH transmission: *rnti, which is transmitted in the* DlData\_PdschSendReq and *DlData\_PdschPayloadTbSendReq messages,*\*\*
* the *pdschScramblingSeqInit, which comes within the* DlData\_PdschSendReq message, see [11432099](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_G7ovvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for details,\*\*
* all scheduling information that comes within the *DlData\_PdschSendReq message, which are elaborated in the following chapters,*
* the PDSCH signal payload: payload, and the associated information: tbIndex, tbFragmentOffset\_bits, and tbFrangment-bytes, which are indicated in the DlData\_PdschPayloadTbSendReq message.

Note, DL PTRS functionality is disabled for FDD and FR1 TDD.

The supported technology types in L1 DL **are shown in table below:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Technology** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor BB** | **Odin BB** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 23R1 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-JA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-JA] |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-LA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-LA] |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 23R1 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-IA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] |
| **FR2-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | - | - | - |
| **FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported | - |

---

#### 4.3.4.1.2 PDSCH Interface for eCPRI-7.2e split

##### 4.3.4.1.2.1 From L1-BB to L1-Radio

###### 4.3.4.1.2.1.1 PDSCH Send Request Message

**4.3.4.1.2.1.1.0-3**  (ID: `12713657`)

PDSCH Send Request Message
Upon reception of *DlData\_PdschSendReq and* DlData\_PdschPayloadTbSendReq messages (See [11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11513002](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRtebfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall encode the PDSCH TB into bit stream.\*\*

 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall build DlDataFH\_PdschSendReq message and send it to RU as encapsulated in the eCPRI protocol, with eCPRI message type 64.

 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall use the info in DlData\_PdschSendReq to generate PDSCH encoded payload and split the encoded payload of one TB into multiple DlDataFH\_PdschEncodedPayloadTbSendReq messages. Each DlDataFH\_PdschEncodedPayloadTbSendReq message carries the whole or partially encoded data of one OFDM symbol (refer [11516899](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSHHFPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for details). Then send DlDataFH\_PdschEncodedPayloadTbSendReq messages to RU as encapsulated in the eCPRI protocol, with eCPRI message type 0.

For more details of *DlDataFH\_PdschSendReq and* DlDataFH\_PdschEncodedPayloadTbSendReq procedure, please refer to [11516796](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSGf_fvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).\*\*

PDSCH processing is handled by 5G-L1-DL(BB) and 5G-L1-DL(Radio) separately. The following table shows the PDSCH/DMRS transmission processing chain in 5G-L1-DL(BB).

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| PDSCH processing chain (FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI) | 5G-L1-DL (BB) |
| PDSCH CRC attachment ([11431793](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_GUjvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Supported (LENC) |
| PDSCH LDPC encoding ([11431880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_G7kvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Supported (LENC) |
| PDSCH scrambling ([11432099](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_G7ovvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Supported (LENC) |

The supported technology types in L1 DL are shown in table below:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Technology** | **Thor BB** | **Odin BB** |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI** | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-GA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-GA] |

Note: Refer to [11466279](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fBeTGPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for 5G-L1-DL (Radio) details.

---

###### 4.3.4.1.2.1.2 DlData_UePairingReq message

###### 4.3.4.1.2.1.2.0-1 DlData_UePairingReq message DlData_UePairingReq message reception (ID: 12657782)

**4.3.4.1.2.1.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12657783`)

DlData\_UePairingReq message
Upon reception of *DlData\_UePairingReq messages ([11513147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRusiPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)), 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall build* DlDataFH\_UePairingReq message ([11517012](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSIVIPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)) and send it to RU as encapsulated in the eCPRI protocol, with eCPRI message type 64.\*\*

For more details of *DlData\_UePairingReq procedure, please refer to ([11513147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRusiPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)).*

---

###### 4.3.4.1.2.1.2.0-2 DlData_UePairingReq message DlData_UePairingReq message validation (ID: 12657784)

**4.3.4.1.2.1.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12657785`)

DlData\_UePairingReq message
Upon reception of the DlData\_UePairingReq message in one slot, if one of following exception cases is met, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the DlData\_UePairingReq message and log the issue:

* Exception Case: DlData\_UePairingReq message arrived out of the reception window defined in [12582684](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3FWjYTSFEfCKaNlcHOQWmg?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw).
* Exception Case: DlData\_UePairingReq message arrived later than DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw message in the slot.

---

##### 4.3.4.1.2.2 bundle

**4.3.4.1.2.2.0-1**  (ID: `11429500`)

bundle
[Before CB011026-A-UPD001]

L1 DL needs to bundle N grants from messages DlData\_PdschSendReq  into one DlDataFH\_PdschSendReq. 'N' can be:

 4: If 'endMark' in all messages DlData\_PdschSendReq  is FALSE;

 Any value between 1...4: If 'endMark' in one DlData\_PdschSendReq  is TRUE or reception window for DlData\_PdschSendReq is closed.

[CB011026-A-UPD001]

Following requirement for PDSCH bundle is descoped in Post FS2 update. Related bundle will be implemented in L2 in the future.

[End CB011026-A-UPD001]

---

##### 4.3.4.1.2.3 From L1-Radio to L1-BB (Send Response Message)

###### 4.3.4.1.2.3.1 Thor BB

**4.3.4.1.2.3.1.0-1**  (ID: `11429527`)

Thor BB
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall support the reception of one or multiple  fragments for message DlDataFH\_PdschSendRespPs (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_15047\_replaced([11519649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSYz0vvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and creation of response message DlData\_PdschSendRespPs message (5G\_L1\_IF\_15405\_replaced([11513016](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRtecPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) per Ue.

Generation of DlData\_PdschSendRespPs, from one or more fragments is as follows:

* The common IEs in DlData\_PdschSendRespPs  are retrieved from the DlDataFH\_PdschSendRespPs fragment common IEs.
* The UE dedicated IEs in DlData\_PdschSendRespPs message are retrieved from each fragment's PdschRespPsGrant structure (5G\_L1\_IF\_15411\_replaced([11516880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSHHDPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* The report order of EIRP expansion coefficient is described in 5G\_L1\_16831\_replaced([11429549](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e-_m1_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Once creation of DlData\_PdschSendRespPs from DlDataFH\_PdschSendRespPs is completed, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall send message to 5G-L2-PS.

---

###### 4.3.4.1.2.3.2 Fragment Loss

**4.3.4.1.2.3.2.0-1**  (ID: `11429542`)

Fragment Loss
Per UE, 5G-L1-DL (BB) will start the timer on receipt of the first fragment of DlDataFH\_PdschSendRespPs message ([11400090](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e87xUfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), any other fragment of *DlDataFH\_PdschSendRespPs not reaching 5G-L1-DL (BB) within the period of timer is considered lost.*

**Note: the design and maintainance of this timer is per SW preference.**

For the generation of *DlData\_PdschSendRespPs message ([11513016](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRtecPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to L2, 5G\_L1-DL (BB) shall include all fragments (the total number of fragments per UE is equal to* numOfLayers ) of *DlDataFH\_PdschSendRespPs ([11519649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSYz0vvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) reaching 5G-L1-DL (BB) within the reception window defined in ([11590275](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BR4mRvwGEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), that is, fragments not considered lost.*\*\*

If any fragments of the UE are lost, 5G-L1-DL (BB) takes no action and no *DlData\_PdschSendRespPs message for this UE is sent to L2-PS.*

---

###### 4.3.4.1.2.3.3 EIRP report order

**4.3.4.1.2.3.3.0-1**  (ID: `11429557`)

EIRP report order
The order of EIRP (Equivalent Isotropic Radiated Power) expansion coefficient ([11468106](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fBndC_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in *eirpCoefficients of* DlData\_PdschSendRespPs message ([11513016](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRtecPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is as bleow:\*\*

* M: *numOfLayers in* DlDataFH\_PdschSendReq message([11516826](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSHHAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | layerInder | *eirpCoefficients* |
| *numOfLayers* | layerIndex 0 | eirpCoefficients of layerIndex 0 |
| layerIndex 1 | eirpCoefficients of layerIndex 1 |  |
| … | … |  |
| layerIdex M-1 | eirpCoefficients of layerIndex M-1 |  |

---

#### 4.3.4.1.3 DlData_PdschSendReqL1sw message validation

##### 4.3.4.1.3.0-2 DlData_PdschSendReqL1sw message validation EnhCtrlChannelTransPrecoding validation for CL 4x4 or 4x2 MIMO (ID: 11429589)

**4.3.4.1.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11429596`)

DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw message validation
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that IE *closedLoop3gppCodebook in* DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw message (5G\_L1\_IF\_87\_replaced([11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) can be 3 "EnhCtrlChannelTransPrecoding" only when all the following conditions are met.\*\*

* *dlMimoMode in* DlCell\_SetupReq message (5G\_L1\_IF\_16\_replaced([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to "CL 4x4 or 4x2 MIMO".\*\*
* *actBeamforming in* DlCell\_SetupReq message (5G\_L1\_IF\_16\_replaced([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to False.\*\*
* *spatialMode in* DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw message (5G\_L1\_IF\_87\_replaced([11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to 2 “closed loop spatial multiplexing”.\*\*
* numOfLayers in DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw message (5G\_L1\_IF\_87\_replaced([11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to 1.

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.1 Codebook index validation

###### 4.3.4.1.3.1.1 Checker 1

**4.3.4.1.3.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `12639051`)

Checker 1
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that codebookIndex is not greater than 3 in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* dlMimoMode = "CL 4x4 or 4x2 MIMO" or “CL 8x4 MIMO”
* ”spatialMode” = ”closed loop spatial multiplexing”
* numStreamIndex” = 2
* “numOfLayers” = 1,

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.1.2 Checker 2

**4.3.4.1.3.1.2.0-1**  (ID: `12713985`)

Checker 2
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that codebookIndex shall be not greater than 2  in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* dlMimoMode is not 8x4 DL MIMO
* ”spatialMode” = ”closed loop spatial multiplexing”,
* “numStreamIndex” = 2,
* “numOfLayers” = 2,

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.1.3 Checker 3

**4.3.4.1.3.1.3.0-1**  (ID: `12714005`)

Checker 3
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that codebookIndex shall be not greater than 1  in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* dlMimoMode =  8x4 DL MIMO
* ”spatialMode” = ”closed loop spatial multiplexing”,
* “numStreamIndex” = 2,
* “numOfLayers” = 2,

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

* “dlMimoMode” = "CL 8x4 MIMO" and
* ”spatialMode” = ”closed loop spatial multiplexing” and
* “numStreamIndex” = 4 and
* "closedLoop3gppCodebook" = "4portTypeICodebook"**,**

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

* “dlMimoMode” = "CL 8x4 MIMO" and

* “numStreamIndex” = 4 and

##### 4.3.4.1.3.4 pdschPrecodingOption4x4 validation

###### 4.3.4.1.3.4.1 Checker 1

**4.3.4.1.3.4.1.0-1**  (ID: `12714746`)

Checker 1
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that pdschPrecodingOption4x4  is not greater than 6 (PortSelection) in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* “dlMimoMode” = "CL 8x4 MIMO" and
* "spatialMode” = ”closed loop spatial multiplexing” and
* "closedLoop3gppCodebook" = "4portTypeICodebook"

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.4.2 Checker 2

**4.3.4.1.3.4.2.0-1**  (ID: `12714801`)

Checker 2
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that pdschPrecodingOption4x4  is equal to 0 (NoPortSelection) in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* “dlMimoMode” = "CL 8x4 MIMO" and
* "spatialMode” = ”closed loop spatial multiplexing” and
* "closedLoop3gppCodebook" = "8portTypeICodebook"

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.4.3 Checker 3

###### 4.3.4.1.3.4.3.0-1 Checker 3 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that pdschPrecodingOption4x4  is equal to 0 in case DlData_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with: “dlMimoMode” = "32x4 DL MIMO" In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error (ID: 12827671)

##### 4.3.4.1.3.5 polarizationSelection validation

**4.3.4.1.3.5.0-1**  (ID: `12714794`)

polarizationSelection validation
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that polarizationSelection is not set to 0 (firstPolarization) or 1 (secondPolarization) in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* ”conformanceTestMode” = False

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.6 open Loop Scheme Validation

###### 4.3.4.1.3.6.1 Checker 1

**4.3.4.1.3.6.1.0-1**  (ID: `12714854`)

Checker 1
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that openLoopScheme is set to EnhCtrlChannelTransPrecoding only in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* dlMimoMode = {"OL 4x4 or 4x2 MIMO"} and
* numStreamIndex = 4

In case invalid value is received 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.6.2 Checker 2

**4.3.4.1.3.6.2.0-1**  (ID: `12714795`)

Checker 2
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that openLoopScheme is set not set to INVALID\_255 or other value outside following set:

* "No Precoding",
* "SCDD",
* "LCDD",
* **"IdentityCodebook"**,\*\*\*\*
* "EnhCtrlChannelTransPrecoding

in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* ”spatialMode” =“Open loop spatial multiplexing”

In case invalid value is received 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.6.3 Checker 3

**4.3.4.1.3.6.3.0-1**  (ID: `12714826`)

Checker 3
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that openLoopScheme is not set to EnhCtrlChannelTransPrecoding in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* actBeamforming = True or,
* numOfLayers > 1,

In case invalid value is received 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.7 isLowPaprOptimizedPrecoding validation

**4.3.4.1.3.7.0-1**  (ID: `12714796`)

isLowPaprOptimizedPrecoding validation
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that ”isLowPaprOptimizedPrecoding” is not set to True in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* ”dlMimoMode” = 0 (CL 2x2 MIMO) or 2 (OL 2x2 MIMO)

In case invalid value is received 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.8 pdschBundleSize validation

**4.3.4.1.3.8.0-1**  (ID: `12714798`)

pdschBundleSize validation
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that ”pdschBundleSize” is set to 4 in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* ”spatialMode” = ”open loop spatial multiplexing” or
* "isLowPaprOptimizedPrecoding” = True,

In case invalid value is received 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.9 closedLoop3gppCodebook validation

###### 4.3.4.1.3.9.1 Checker 1

**4.3.4.1.3.9.1.0-2**  (ID: `12827690`)

Checker 1
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that ”closedLoop3gppCodebook” is set only to any of the following values:

* [CB008577-B]"32portTypeICodebook",[End CB008577-B]
* "8portTypeICodebook",
* "4portTypeICodebook",
* **"IdentityCodebook"**,\*\*\*\*
* **"EnhCtrlChannelTransPrecoding"**

in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* ”spatialMode” = "closed loop spatial multiplexing" and
* numStreamIndex = 4

In case invalid value is received 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.9.2 Checker 2

**4.3.4.1.3.9.2.0-1**  (ID: `12714912`)

Checker 2
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that ”closedLoop3gppCodebook” is set only to any of the following values:

* "2portTypeICodebook",
* "IdentityCodebook";

in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* ”spatialMode” = "closed loop spatial multiplexing" and
* numStreamIndex = 2

In case invalid value is received 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.9.3 Checker 3

**4.3.4.1.3.9.3.0-1**  (ID: `12714919`)

Checker 3
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that ”closedLoop3gppCodebook” is set to "INVALID"

in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* ”spatialMode” = "open loop spatial multiplexing" or "Tx div","single antenna",

In case invalid value is received 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.9.4 Checker 4

**4.3.4.1.3.9.4.0-1**  (ID: `12714920`)

Checker 4
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that ”closedLoop3gppCodebook” is not set to 3 "EnhCtrlChannelTransPrecoding"

in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* actBeamforming = True or
* numOfLayers > 1

In case invalid value is received 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.9.5 Checker 5

**4.3.4.1.3.9.5.0-1**  (ID: `12714921`)

Checker 5
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that ”closedLoop3gppCodebook” is not set to 3 "EnhCtrlChannelTransPrecoding"

in case DlData\_pdschSendReq was received for subcell with:

* actBeamforming = True or
* numOfLayers > 1

In case invalid value is received 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11512828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DhtV0PvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.10 Antenna ports validation

###### 4.3.4.1.3.10.1 Checker 1

**4.3.4.1.3.10.1.0-1**  (ID: `12713655`)

Checker 1
If “numOfLayers” = 1, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate if antPort is not set to 1001, 1002 or 1003.

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DC-ydvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.10.2 Checker 2

**4.3.4.1.3.10.2.0-1**  (ID: `12713675`)

Checker 2
If “numOfLayers” = 2, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate if antPort is not set to 1002 or 1003.

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DC-ydvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.11 Spatial Mode Validation

###### 4.3.4.1.3.11.1 Checker 1

**4.3.4.1.3.11.1.0-1**  (ID: `12713677`)

Checker 1
If “dlMimoMode” = “1x1 Single Antenna”, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate if “spatialMode” is not equal to:

* “Tx div”
* “closed loop spatial multiplexing”
* “open loop spatial multiplexing”

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DC-ydvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.11.2 Checker 2

**4.3.4.1.3.11.2.0-1**  (ID: `12713240`)

Checker 2
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate if spatialMode value is not set to:

* "single antenna"
* "Tx div"
* "open loop spatial multiplexing"

for subcells with "dlMimoMode" set to  "CL 8x4 MIMO".

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DC-ydvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.11.3 Checker 3

**4.3.4.1.3.11.3.0-1**  (ID: `12713244`)

Checker 3
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate if "spatialMode" is not set to "Tx div" value for subcells with dlMimoMode set to "CL 2x2 MIMO"

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DC-ydvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.11.4 Checker 4

**4.3.4.1.3.11.4.0-1**  (ID: `12713245`)

Checker 4
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate if "spatialMode" is not set to any of following values:

* "Tx div"
* "closed loop spatial multiplexing"

for subcells with dlMimoMode value set to "OL 2x2 MIMO"

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DC-ydvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.11.5 Checker 5

**4.3.4.1.3.11.5.0-1**  (ID: `12713247`)

Checker 5
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate if "spatialMode" value is not set to  "Tx div"  for subcells with "dlMimoMode " set to "CL 4x4 or 4x2 MIMO"

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DC-ydvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.11.6 Checker 6

**4.3.4.1.3.11.6.0-1**  (ID: `12713246`)

Checker 6
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate if "spatialMode" value is not set to any of following values:

* "Tx div"
* "closed loop spatial multiplexing"

for subcells with "dlMimoMode" set to "OL 4x4 or 4x2 MIMO".

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DC-ydvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.12 Number of Dmrs Cdm Group Without Data Validation

###### 4.3.4.1.3.12.1 Checker 1

**4.3.4.1.3.12.1.0-1**  (ID: `12713975`)

Checker 1
If “numOfLayers” = 3 or 4 or “dlDmrsLen” = 2,  5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate if “numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData” is not equal to 1.

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition 11512803

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.12.2 Checker 2

**4.3.4.1.3.12.2.0-1**  (ID: `12713978`)

Checker 2
If “dlDmrsConfigType” = 1,  5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate if “numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData” is not equal to 3.

In that case 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition 11512803

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.13 PDSCH startSymbol validation

###### 4.3.4.1.3.13.1 Checker 1

**4.3.4.1.3.13.1.0-1**  (ID: `12718618`)

Checker 1
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that startSymbol from DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw is not higher than dlDmrsTypeAPos from the same message.

In case  startSymbol has higher value, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition 11512851

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.13.2 Checker 2

**4.3.4.1.3.13.2.0-1**  (ID: `12718760`)

Checker 2
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that startSymbol from DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw is not higher or equal to (14 - numOfPdschSymbols) from the same message

In case  startSymbol has higher value, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition 11512851

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.14 dlDmrsTypeAPos validation

**4.3.4.1.3.14.0-1**  (ID: `12718765`)

dlDmrsTypeAPos validation
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that dlDmrsTypeAPos from DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw is not bigger than startSymbol + numOfPdschSymbols - 1.

In case startSymbol has lower value, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition 11512851

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.15 startPrb validation

**4.3.4.1.3.15.0-1**  (ID: `12718826`)

startPrb validation
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that startPrb from DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw is not higher than dlBandwidth recieved for given cell in DlCell\_PdschSendReqL1sw minus numOfPrb from the same massage.

NOT: “startPrb” + “numOfPrb” > “dlBandwidth”;

In case startPrb has higher value, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition 11512851

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.16 rbgSizeFirst validation

**4.3.4.1.3.16.0-1**  (ID: `12718835`)

rbgSizeFirst validation
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that rbgSizeFirst from DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw is not higher than rbgSize recieved from the same massage.

In case rbgSizeFirst has higher value, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition 11512851

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.17 unscheduledAreaStartPrb validation

###### 4.3.4.1.3.17.1 Checker 1

**4.3.4.1.3.17.1.0-1**  (ID: `12718919`)

Checker 1
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that unscheduledAreaStartPrb from DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw is not smaller than startPrb from the same massage.

In case unscheduledAreaStartPrb  is smaller, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512929](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_EpePIvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.17.2 Checker 2

**4.3.4.1.3.17.2.0-1**  (ID: `12718927`)

Checker 2
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that unscheduledAreaStartPrb and unscheduledAreaNumOfPrb if their sum minus 1 doesn't exceed the last PRB index scheduled for this grant.

NOT: “unscheduledAreaStartPrb”+“unscheduledAreaNumOfPrb” -1 > the last PRB scheduled for this grant;

In case the sum exceeds the last PRB index scheduled for this grant, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512929](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_EpePIvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.18 unscheduledAreaStartSymbol validation

**4.3.4.1.3.18.0-1**  (ID: `12718948`)

unscheduledAreaStartSymbol validation
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that unscheduledAreaStartSymbol from DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw is not smaller than startSymbol from the same massage.

In case unscheduledAreaStartSymbol is smaller, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512929](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_EpePIvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.19 unscheduledAreaNumOfSymbols validation

###### 4.3.4.1.3.19.1 Checker 1

**4.3.4.1.3.19.1.0-1**  (ID: `12718950`)

Checker 1
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that unscheduledAreaNumOfSymbols from DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw is not higher than numOfPdschSymbols from the same massage.

In case unscheduledAreaNumOfSymbols is higher, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512929](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_EpePIvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.19.2 Checker 2

**4.3.4.1.3.19.2.0-1**  (ID: `12718841`)

Checker 2
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that rbgSizeFirst is not equal to 1 in case ”conformanceTestMode” received for given subcell in DlCell\_PdschSendReqL1sw is False

In case rbgSizeFirst is 1 for such subcell, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512851](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_DytmQPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.20 rtBfUeIndex validation

**4.3.4.1.3.20.0-1**  (ID: `12720477`)

rtBfUeIndex validation
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that group of bits with indexes (counting from MSB) from 0 to 3 in rtBfUeIndex are equal to 1 (0b0001)  in case eirpEnable received in the same message is 1.

In case rtBfUeIndex MSB bits 0~3 are not set to 0x0001 for such case, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition 11512951

---

##### 4.3.4.1.3.21 numStreamIndex validation

###### 4.3.4.1.3.21.1 Checker 1

**4.3.4.1.3.21.1.0-1**  (ID: `12720634`)

Checker 1
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that numStreamIndex is equal to 4  if DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw was received for subcell with following configuration:

* "closedLoop3gppCodebook" = "8portTypeICodebook" or "4portTypeICodebook",

In case numStreamIndex is not equal to 4 in such configuration, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition 11512951

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.21.2 Checker 2

**4.3.4.1.3.21.2.0-1**  (ID: `12720632`)

Checker 2
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that numStreamIndex is not equal to 2 for FDD FR1 case if "dlMimoMode” = "OL 4x4 or 4x2 MIMO" or "CL 4x4 or 4x2 MIMO",

In case numStreamIndex is equal to 2 in such configuration, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512969](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_FEpvQfvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.21.3 Checker 3

**4.3.4.1.3.21.3.0-1**  (ID: `12720537`)

Checker 3
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that numStreamIndex is not set to 4, in case DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw was received for subcell with “dlMimoMode” = "CL 2x2 MIMO" or “OL 2x2 MIMO”,

In case numStreamIndex is set to 4 fur such subcell, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512969](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_FEpvQfvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.21.4 Checker 4

**4.3.4.1.3.21.4.0-1**  (ID: `12720549`)

Checker 4
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that numStreamIndex is not smaller numOfLayers from DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw,

In case numStreamIndex is smaller than numOfLayers , 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512969](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_FEpvQfvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 4.3.4.1.3.21.5 Checker 5

**4.3.4.1.3.21.5.0-1**  (ID: `12720633`)

Checker 5
5G-L1-DL (BB) shall validate that numStreamIndex is equal to 2  if DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw was received for subcell with following configuration:

* "closedLoop3gppCodebook" = "2portTypeICodebook",

In case numStreamIndex is not equal to 2 in such configuration, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11512969](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_FEpvQfvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

### 4.3.4.2 PDSCH Time Domain resource allocation

#### 4.3.4.2.1 PDSCH Time Domain resource allocation Overview

**4.3.4.2.1.0-4**  (ID: `12713862`)

PDSCH Time Domain resource allocation Overview
5G-L1-DL shall identify the PDSCH time domain allocation with the parameters received in the *DlData\_PdschSendReq message (See [11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):*

The first ODFM symbol number in the slot allocated to the PDSCH: *startSymbol.*

Number of consecutive OFDM symbols for the transmission: *numOfPdschSymbols*

The time domain data are used to identify the number of additional DM-RS if needed.

Please refer to [11515357](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tULU_Pv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the supported *startSymbol  and* numOfPdschSymbols for SIB1.\*\*

The supported technology types in L1 DL **are shown in table below:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Technology** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor BB** | **Odin BB** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 23R1 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-JA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-JA] |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-LA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-LA] |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-IA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI** | - | - | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-GA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-GA] |
| **FR2-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | - | - | - |
| **FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported | - |

---

#### 4.3.4.2.2 List of supported start symbols

##### 4.3.4.2.2.0-2 List of supported start symbols For Loner (ID: 11429651)

**4.3.4.2.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11429659`)

List of supported start symbols
For Loner

Values from this table match the startSymbol from pdschAllocationConfig (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_925G\_L1\_IF\_93\_replaced([11512851](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRsQT_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), part of DlData\_PdschSendReq message) and indicate the start OFDM symbol used by UE in given slot.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Technology | PDSCH start symbol (startSymbol) | First Release |
| FDD | {0, 1, 2, 3} Note: value 0 is for conformance test only | Inherited |
| TDD FR1 | {0, 1, 2} Note: value 0 is for conformance test only | Inherited |
| [Before CB009007-A] {0, 1, 2} [CB009007-A] {0, 1, 2, 3} [End CB009007-A] Note: value 0 is for conformance test only | 23R1-SR |  |
| TDD FR2 | {0, 1, 2} Note: value 0 is for conformance test only | Inherited |

(Values in the table are implicitly cumulative within FR)

---

##### 4.3.4.2.2.0-3 List of supported start symbols For Loki (ID: 11429668)

**4.3.4.2.2.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11429703`)

List of supported start symbols
For Loki L1 HW

Values from this table match the startSymbol from pdschAllocationConfig (5G\_L1\_IF\_93\_replaced([11512851](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRsQT_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), part of DlData\_PdschSendReq message) and indicate the start OFDM symbol used by UE in given slot.

The supported startSymbol are listed in table below:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Technology | startSymbol |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | {0, 1, 2, 3}   Note: value 0 is for conformance test only |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI |  |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI |  |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | {0, 1, 2, 3}   [Before CB008370-AA] Note: value 0 is for conformance test only [End CB008370-AA] |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI |  |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | {0, 1, 2}   Note: value 0 is for conformance test only |

---

##### 4.3.4.2.2.0-4 List of supported start symbols For Thor BB (ID: 11429712)

**4.3.4.2.2.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `11429744`)

List of supported start symbols
Values from this table match the *startSymbol from* pdschAllocationConfig (5G\_L1\_IF\_93\_replaced([11512851](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRsQT_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), part of *DlData\_PdschSendReq message) and indicate the start OFDM symbol used by UE in given slot.*\*\*

The supported *startSymbol are listed in table below:*

### 4.3.4.3 Frequency Domain resource allocation

#### 4.3.4.3.1 Frequency Domain resource allocation Overview

**4.3.4.3.1.0-4**  (ID: `12714991`)

Frequency Domain resource allocation Overview
5G-L1-DL shall identify the PDSCH frequency domain allocation with the parameters received in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message (See [11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

-- RBG bitmap: rat0Bitmap

-- starting PRB: startPrb

-- size of RBGs besides the first and last RBG allocated to UE: rbgSize,

-- size of the first RBG allocated to UE: rbgSizeFirst

-- number of PRBs: numOfPrb.

When resource allocation type 0 is used, 5G-L1-DL shall determine RBG as illustrated in ([11429941](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_BcCfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When resource allocation type 1 is used, all bits of rat0Bitmap shall be set to 0.

The supported technology types in L1 DL **are shown in table below:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Technology** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor BB** | **Odin BB** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 23R1 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-JA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-JA] |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-LA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-LA] |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-IA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI** | - | - | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-GA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-GA] |
| **FR2-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | - | - | - |
| **FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported | - |

---

Supported RAT configurations
RAT0 - RBG size and frequency allocation

Resource Allocation Type 0 shall be used for PDSCH along with following parameters :

* RBG bitmap: rat0Bitmap,
* starting PRB: startPrb,
* size of RBGs besides the first and last RBG allocated to UE: rbgSize,
* size of the first RBG allocated to UE: rbgSizeFirst
* number of PRBs: numOfPrb.

The RBGs shall be indexed in the order of increasing frequency and starting at the lowest frequency of the bandwidth part. The order of RBG bitmap is such that RBG 0 to RBG NRBG-1 are mapped from MSB to LSB. The RBG is allocated to the UE if the corresponding bit value in the bitmap is 1, otherwise the RBG is not allocated to the UE. Bit31 to bit13 are used now, bit12 to bit0 are reserved. In common cases, the used bit shall be set to 1 for default BWP.

When the IE rat0Bitmap is not equal to ‘0’  (i.e. one or more bits are set to ‘1’ ), 5G-L1-DL shall determine the 1st PRB and the size of each allocated RBG (i.e. each bit in rat0Bitmap set to '1') as follows. Let’s denote N the number of bits set to '1' and RBG\_i the ith RBG with i = 0…N-1.

- RBG\_0:

o Start\_PRB (0) = startPrb

o RBG size (0) = rbgSizeFirst

- RBG\_i (if N>=3, then middle RBG exist, i=1…N-2):

o Start\_PRB (i) = Start\_PRB\_(i-1) + RBG size(i-1) + NonAllocRBG (i) x rbgSize

o RBG size (i) = rbgSize

- RBG\_N-1 (if N>=2, then last RBG exist):

o Start\_PRB (N-1) = Start\_PRB\_(N-2) + RBG size(N-2) + NonAllocRBG (N-1) x rbgSize

o RBG size (N-1) = numOfPrb – (rbgSizeFirst + rbgSize x (N-2))

With:

- startPrb is the first PRB of the first allocated RBG. numOfPrbs is the total number of PRBs in all allocated RBGs.

- NonAllocRBG (i) stands for the number of non-allocated RBGs between RBG i and i-1. It corresponds the number of bits set at 0 between the bit i and bit i-1 set to 1

Example 1:

rat0Bitmap = '0011010110' -> N = 5

startPrb = 16

numOfPrbs = 20

rbgSize = 4

rbgSizeFirst = 4

- RBG\_0:

o          Start\_PRB (0) = 16

o          RBG size (0) = 4

-           RBG\_1

o          Start\_PRB (1) = 16 + 4 + 0 x 4 = 20

o          RBG size (1) = 4

-           RBG\_2

o          Start\_PRB (2) = 20 + 4 + 1 x 4 = 28

o          RBG size (2) = 4

-           RBG\_3

o          Start\_PRB (3) = 28 + 4 + 1 x 4 = 36

o          RBG size (3) = 4

-           RBG\_4

o          Start\_PRB (4) = 36 + 4 + 0 x 4 = 40

o          RBG size (4) = 20 - (4+ 4 x max (5-2,0)) = 4.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating a concept related to resource allocation, likely in a telecommunications context, labeled "Example 1". The diagram is structured with several rows and columns, representing different parameters and their values.
The top row is a numerical scale from 0 to 50, labeled "CRB". Below this, a row labeled "BWP" spans across a significant portion of the diagram. Beneath "BWP", there are ten boxes labeled "RBGO" through "RBG9", each corresponding to a range of numbers on the CRB scale. Specifically:
- RBGO covers 0-3
- RBG1 covers 4-7
- RBG2 covers 8-11
- RBG3 covers 12-15
- RBG4 covers 16-19
- RBG5 covers 20-23
- RBG6 covers 24-27
- RBG7 covers 28-31
- RBG8 covers 32-35
- RBG9 covers 36-39
Below the RBG labels, there is another row of numbers from 0 to 39, aligning with the respective RBG boxes.
The next row is labeled "ratoBitmap" and contains binary values (0s and 1s) within cells that correspond to the RBG ranges. The values are:
- 0 for RBGO (0-3)
- 0 for RBG1 (4-7)
- 1 for RBG2 (8-11)
- 1 for RBG3 (12-15)
- 0 for RBG4 (16-19)
- 1 for RBG5 (20-23)
- 0 for RBG6 (24-27)
- 1 for RBG7 (28-31)
- 1 for RBG8 (32-35)
- 0 for RBG9 (36-39)
The bottom part of the diagram shows annotations related to "startPrb" and "RBG size".
- "startPrb=16" is indicated by a horizontal arrow pointing from the left to the CRB scale at position 16.
- Several "RBG size" annotations are present, with arrows pointing to specific segments of the CRB scale. These annotations also include formulas and calculated values:
- "RBG size (0) =rbgSizeFirst =4" points to the range 8-11.
- "RBG size (1) =rbgSize =4" points to the range 12-15.
- "RBG size (2) =rbgSize =4" points to the range 20-23.
- "RBG size (3) =rbgSize =4" points to the range 28-31.
- "RBG size (4) =numOfPrb - rbgSizeFirst - rbgSize*3 =20-4-4*3 =4" points to the range 36-39.
The cells in the "ratoBitmap" row are colored green when the value is 1, and white when the value is 0.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3973.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Example 2:

rat0Bitmap = '1011010111' -> N = 7

startPrb = 9

numOfPrbs = 27

rbgSize = 4

rbgSizeFirst = 3

- RBG\_0:

o          Start\_PRB (0) = 9

o          RBG size (0) = 3

-           RBG\_1

o          Start\_PRB (1) = 9 + 3 + 1 x 4 = 16

o          RBG size (1) = 4

-           RBG\_2

o          Start\_PRB (2) = 16 + 4 + 0 x 4 = 20

o          RBG size (2) = 4

-           RBG\_3

o          Start\_PRB (3) = 20 + 4 + 1 x 4 = 28

o          RBG size (3) = 4

-           RBG\_4

o          Start\_PRB (4) = 28 + 4 + 1 x 4 = 36

o          RBG size (3) = 4

-           RBG\_5

o          Start\_PRB (5) = 36 + 4 + 0 x 4 = 40

o          RBG size (5) = 4

-           RBG\_6

o          Start\_PRB (6) = 40 + 4 + 0 x 4 = 44

o          RBG size (6) = 27 - (3+ 4 x max (5,0)) = 4.

At the top, there's a row labeled "CRB" with numbers from 0 to 50, representing indices or positions. Below this, a gray bar is labeled "BWP". Underneath "BWP", there are ten boxes labeled "RBGO" through "RBG9", each containing a range of numbers. For example, "RBGO" covers numbers 0 to 2, "RBG1" covers 3 to 5, and so on, up to "RBG9" covering 36 to 38.
Below the "RBGO" to "RBG9" row, there's a row labeled "rat0Bitmap" with a sequence of 0s and 1s, visually represented by colored cells (green for 1, white for 0). This sequence is aligned with the "RBGO" to "RBG9" boxes.
Further down, there are annotations indicating "startPrb=9" and various "RBG size" calculations. The calculated values for each RBG size are also provided.

| \* **startPrb**: 9 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **RBG size (0)**: =rbgSizeFirst = 3 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **RBG size (1)**: =rbgSize = 4 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **RBG size (2)**: =rbgSize = 4 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **RBG size (3)**: =rbgSize = 4 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **RBG size (4)**: =rbgSize = 4 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **RBG size (5)**: =rbgSize = 4 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **RBG size (6)**: =numOfPrb - rbgSizeFirst - rbgSize*5 = 27-3-4*5 = 4[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3973.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

[5GC001868-A] 5G-L1-DL shall also support RAT0, with RBG size and frequency allocation are determined as explained above in Loki L1 HW [End 5GC001868-A]

[5GC001972-A] 5G-L1-DL shall also support RAT0, with continuous RBG frequency allocation pattern in vDU L1 HW.  Note: Since DL FDM scheduling will not be introduced in L3 call phase, 5G-L2-PS will actually allocate continuous RBG. [End 5GC001972-A]

[RP003187-A, RP003187-H] L1 shall also support RAT0, with RBG size and frequency allocation are determined as explained above in Thor for FR1 TDD eCPRI and FR1 FDD CPRI . [End RP003187-A, RP003187-H]

[CB008313-B] 5G-L1-DL shall also support RAT0, with RBG size and frequency allocation are determined as explained above in ASOE for FDD CPRI and TDD FR1 CPRI. [End CB008313-B]

[CB007814-A] 5G-L1-DL shall also support RAT0, with RBG size and frequency allocation are determined as explained above in ABIO for FR1 FDD eCPRI. [End CB007814-A]

[CB008205-B, CB008205-C] 5G-L1-DL shall also support requirements above in ABIO for O-RAN compliant FR1 FDD eCPRI. [End CB008205-B, CB008205-C]

[Before CNI-122313]

[CB009443] 5G-L1-DL shall also support SSB Case C for FR1 TDD eCPRI in RINLINE1. [End CB009443]

[CB009858-C] 5G-L1-DL shall also support SSB Case A for FR1 FDD eCPRI in RINLINE1. [End CB009858-C]

[CNI-122313] RINLINE 1 support is terminated in 24R1, requirement is not valid for RINLINE 1 starting from 24R2 [End CNI-122313]

#### 4.3.4.3.5 Multiplexing with other channels in frequency domain

##### 4.3.4.3.5.1 FDM with PDCCH

**4.3.4.3.5.1.0-4**  (ID: `11430248`)

FDM with PDCCH
FDM between PDCCH and PDSCH in the same symbol shall be supported in TDD FR1. Refer to 5G\_L1\_15131\_replaced([11440838](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_dg7vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Technology** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** BB\*\*\*\* |
| **FR1-TDD-CPRI** | **-** | Supported | Supported |
| **FR1-TDD-eCPRI -7-2a** | **-** | Supported | Supported |
| **FR1-TDD-eCPRI -7-2e** | **-** | **-** | **[CB009936-F]** Supported **[End CB009936-F]** |

**[CB009936-F]**

Note:

1. Parity from CB008367: FDM between PDCCH and PDSCH in the same symbol 1 is supported in FR1-TDD-eCPRI-7-2e.
2. Parity from CB008370: FDM between PDCCH and PDSCH in the same symbol 0 is supported in FR1-TDD-eCPRI-7-2e.

**[End CB009936-F]**

---

## 4.3.5 PDSCH DMRS

### 4.3.5.1 DMRS sequence generation

#### 4.3.5.1.0-1 DMRS sequence generation Overview (ID: 11430272)

**4.3.5.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12715481`)

DMRS sequence generation
5G-L1-DL(BB) shall generate the DMRS sequence associated to PDSCH based on the parameters received in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message (See [11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

-- DMRS scrambling sequence initialization parameters NID: *dmrsScramblingSequenceInt*

-- Nscid: *nscId*

5G-L2-PS shall always fill properly the field *dmrsScramblingSequenceInt in the respective message. 5G-L1-DL shall always use the parameter* dmrsScramblingSequenceInt for the sequence scrambling initialization. Its value should be set to *physical-layer cell ID:* physCellId obtained from the *DlCell\_SetupReq message when the UE doesn't have specific scrambling parameters for PDSCH channel.*\*\*\*\*

The initial scrambling sequence can be other values than the physical-layer cell ID, please Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4405\_replaced([11142329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr3wSfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) about 5G-L2-PS settings for *dmrsScramblingSequenceInt and* nscId.\*\*

The supported technology types in L1 DL **are shown in table below:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Technology** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor BB** | **Odin BB** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 23R1 onwards: Supported | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | **[CB012115-SR-IA]** Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported **[End CB012115-SR-IA]** |
| **FR2-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | - | - | - |
| **FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported | - |

---

The reference-signal sequence generation ([image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]), for the front loaded DM-RS and the additional ones, is computed by the L1 as:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in black text on a white background. The equation is:
$r(n) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(1 - 2 \cdot c(2n)) + j\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(1 - 2 \cdot c(2n + 1))$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] is the QPSK modulation of a pseudo random sequence ([image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry close-up of the text "c(n)". The letters and parenthesis are out of focus, with a slight blue and orange tint.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]) resulting from 2 Maximum Length Sequences (MLS).

* The first MLS ([image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of the mathematical expression "x₁(m)". The "x" is on the left, followed by a subscript "1", and then enclosed in parentheses is the letter "m". The colors are muted, with a slight blue and orange tint.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]) is initialized with [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "x₁(0)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5] set to 1, and [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "x₁(m) = 0". The text is in a light blue color against a white background. The expression is written in a standard mathematical font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] with [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains the text "m = 1, 2, ..., 30". The text is in a light gray color against a white background. The characters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7].
* The second MLS ([image-8][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8]) is initialized with the following parameters, received in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message:

[image-9][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$c\_{\text{init}} = (2^{17} (N\_{\text{symb}}^{\text{slot}} n\_{\text{s,f}}^{\mu} + l + 1)(2N\_{\text{ID}}^{n\_{\text{scID}}} + 1) + 2N\_{\text{ID}}^{n\_{\text{scID}}} + n\_{\text{SCID}}) \pmod{2^{31}}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.010.png[/image\_path][/image-9]

- [image-10][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression written in a stylized font with a gradient effect. The expression is "n" with a superscript "μ" and a subscript "eff". The colors used are a blend of orange, yellow, blue, and purple, creating a vibrant and somewhat abstract appearance. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.011.png[/image\_path][/image-10]: The NR-slot number in the radio frame. It is provided with the parameter "slot" in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message.

- [image-11][image\_desc]The image is completely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.012.png[/image\_path][/image-11]: The OFDM symbol number of the DMRS transmission within the NR-slot.

-    [image-12][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.013.png[/image\_path][/image-12]: The index of the scrambling identifier. It is provided by L2 with the parameter "nscId" in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message.

-     [image-13][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4016.014.png[/image\_path][/image-13]: It is directly provided by L2 with the parameter “dmrsScramblingSequenceInt” in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message.

There are two configuration types for the PDSCH DMRS where different sequence range is used for each type. The PDSCH DMRS configuration type is provided to L1 by the parameter “dlDmrsConfigType” in the *DlData\_PdschSendReq (5G\_L1\_IF\_87\_replaced([11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) message for* *non-eCPRI-7-2e or* DlDataFH\_PdschSendReq message (5G\_L1\_IF\_15113\_replaced([11516826](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSHHAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for eCPRI-7-2e.\*\*\*\*

The supported technology types in L1 DL **are shown in table below:**

For DMRS Type 1**:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Technology** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor BB** | **Odin BB** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Supported | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** |
| **FR1-FDD-eCPRI-7-2a** | - | Supported | Supported | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | - | Supported | Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-eCPRI-7-2a** | Supported | Supported | Supported | [CB012115-SR-IA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] |
| **FR2-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | - | - | - |
| **FR2-TDD-eCPRI-7-2a** | Supported | Supported | Supported | - |

For DMRS Type 2:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Technology** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor BB** | **Odin BB** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | - | - | - | - |
| **FR1-FDD-eCPRI-7-2a** | - | - | - | - |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | - | - | - | - |
| **FR1-TDD-CPRI** | - | Supported | Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-eCPRI-7-2a** | - | Supported | Supported | - |
| **FR2-TDD-CPRI** | - | - | - | - |
| **FR2-TDD-eCPRI-7-2a** | - | - | **-** | - |

Configuration Type 1
5G-L1-DL computes several pseudo random sequences [image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4018.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] for the front loaded DM-RS and the additional ones.  Each of them is used to generate the corresponding reference-signal sequence.
 
For each ODFM symbol handling the DM-RS, 5G-L1-DL generates 6 DM-RS symbols per Resource Block allocated to the PDSCH transmission as follow.
[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a system of six equations. Each equation defines a complex number, denoted by $r\_l$, as a function of $RB\_{idx}$. The equations are structured as follows:
$r\_l(6 \times RB\_{idx}) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(12 \times RB\_{idx})) + j \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(12 \times RB\_{idx} + 1))$
$r\_l(6 \times RB\_{idx} + 1) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(12 \times RB\_{idx} + 2)) + j \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(12 \times RB\_{idx} + 3))$
$r\_l(6 \times RB\_{idx} + 2) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(12 \times RB\_{idx} + 4)) + j \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(12 \times RB\_{idx} + 5))$
$r\_l(6 \times RB\_{idx} + 3) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(12 \times RB\_{idx} + 6)) + j \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(12 \times RB\_{idx} + 7))$
$r\_l(6 \times RB\_{idx} + 4) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(12 \times RB\_{idx} + 8)) + j \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(12 \times RB\_{idx} + 9))$
$r\_l(6 \times RB\_{idx} + 5) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(12 \times RB\_{idx} + 10)) + j \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(12 \times RB\_{idx} + 11))$
The equations are enclosed in a large curly brace on the left side, indicating they form a system. The term $j$ represents the imaginary unit. The function $c(\cdot)$ is not defined in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4018.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
When the parameter offsetRbDmrs, received in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message is set to 0, the RB index ([image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "RBidx" in a stylized font with a blue and orange gradient. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4018.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]), in the above formulas, corresponds to the Common Resource Block (CRB) index of the channel bandwidth. Then, for allocation type 1, the RB index ([image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "RBidx" in a stylized font with a blue and orange gradient. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4018.003.png[/image\_path][/image-3]) of the PDSCH allocation is in the following range.
[image-4][image\_desc]startPrb ≤ RBidx ≤ startPrb + numOfPrb[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4018.004.png[/image\_path][/image-4]
When the parameter offsetRbDmrs, received in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message are not equal to 0, the RB index ([image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "RBidx" in a stylized font with a blue and orange gradient. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4018.003.png[/image\_path][/image-5]) corresponds to the PRB index of the initial bandwidth part and is computed as follow.
[image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a stylized font. The equation is: RB\_idx = CRB\_idx - offsetRbD mrs. The text is in a light blue color with a slight gradient effect. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4018.005.png[/image\_path][/image-6]
For the allocation type 1, in the initial bandwidth part, the RB index ([image-7][image\_desc]The image contains the text "RBidx" in a stylized font with a blue and orange gradient. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4018.003.png[/image\_path][/image-7]) of the PDSCH allocation is in the following range.
[image-8][image\_desc]startPrb - offsetRbDmrs <= RBidx <= startPrb - offsetRbDmrs + numOfPrb[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4018.006.png[/image\_path][/image-8]
 
The supported technology types in L1 DL **are shown in table below:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Technology** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor BB** | **Odin BB** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 23R1 onwards: Supported | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: **Supported** | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 23R1 onwards: Supported | **[CB012115-SR-IA]** Starting from 27R2 onwards: Supported **[End CB012115-SR-IA]** |
| **FR2-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | - | - | - |
| **FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported | - |
