5G-L1-DL computes several pseudo random sequences [image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4021.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] for the front loaded DM-RS and the additional ones.  Each of them is used to generate the corresponding reference-signal sequence.
 
For each ODFM symbol handling the DM-RS, 5G-L1-DL generates 4 DM-RS symbols per Resource Block allocated to the PDSCH transmission as follow.
 
      [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a system of four equations. Each equation defines a complex number $r\_i$ as a function of $RB\_{idx}$. The equations are as follows:
$r\_i(4 \times RB\_{idx}) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(8 \times RB\_{idx})) + j \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(8 \times RB\_{idx} + 1))$
$r\_i(4 \times RB\_{idx} + 1) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(8 \times RB\_{idx} + 2)) + j \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(8 \times RB\_{idx} + 3))$
$r\_i(4 \times RB\_{idx} + 2) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(8 \times RB\_{idx} + 4)) + j \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(8 \times RB\_{idx} + 5))$
$r\_i(4 \times RB\_{idx} + 3) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(8 \times RB\_{idx} + 6)) + j \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times (1 - 2 \times c(8 \times RB\_{idx} + 7))$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4021.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
When the parameter offsetRbDmrs, received in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message (5G\_L1\_IF\_87\_replaced([11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for non-eCPRI-7-2e [CB009936-F] or DlDataFH\_PdschSendReq message (5G\_L1\_IF\_15113\_replaced([11516826](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSHHAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for eCPRI-7-2e [End CB009936-F]  is set to 0, the RB index ([image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "RBidx" in a stylized font with a blue and orange gradient. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4021.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]), in the above formulas, corresponds to the Common Resource Block (CRB) index of the channel bandwidth. Then, for allocation type 1, the RB index ([image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "RBidx" in a stylized font with a blue and orange gradient. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4021.003.png[/image\_path][/image-3]) of the PDSCH allocation is in the following range.
[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical inequality. The text reads: `startPrb ≤ RB_idx ≤ startPrb + numOfPrb`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4021.004.png[/image\_path][/image-4]
When the parameter offsetRbDmrs, received in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message (5G\_L1\_IF\_87\_replaced([11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for non-eCPRI-7-2e [CB009936-F] or DlDataFH\_PdschSendReq message (5G\_L1\_IF\_15113\_replaced([11516826](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSHHAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for eCPRI-7-2e [End CB009936-F] are not equal to 0, the RB index ([image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "RBidx" in a stylized font with a blue and orange gradient. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4021.003.png[/image\_path][/image-5]) corresponds to the PRB index of the initial bandwidth part and is computed as follow.
[image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a stylized font. The equation is:
$RB\_{idx} = CRB\_{idx} - offsetRbD mrs$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4021.005.png[/image\_path][/image-6]
For the allocation type 1, in the initial bandwidth part, the RB index ([image-7][image\_desc]The image contains the text "RBidx" in a stylized font with a blue and orange gradient. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4021.003.png[/image\_path][/image-7]) of the PDSCH allocation is in the following range.
[image-8][image\_desc]startPrb - offsetRbDmrs <= RBidx <= startPrb - offsetRbDmrs + numOfPrb[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4021.006.png[/image\_path][/image-8]
 
The supported technology types in L1 DL are shown in table below:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Technology | Loner | Loki | Thor BB | Thor RU |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | - | - | - | - |
| FR1-FDD-eCPRI-7-2a | - | - | - | - |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | - | - | - | - |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | - | Supported | Supported | - |
| FR1-TDD-eCPRI-7-2a | - | Supported | Supported | - |
| FR1-TDD-eCPRI-7-2e | - | - | - | [CB009936-F] Supported [End CB009936-F] |
| FR2-TDD-CPRI | - | - | - | - |
| FR2-TDD-eCPRI-7-2a | - | - | - | - |

---

#### 4.3.5.1.0-3 DMRS sequence generation DMRS power (ID: 11430675)

**4.3.5.1.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `12717605`)

DMRS sequence generation
The power of DMRS REs depends on the parameters *numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData and* dlDmrsConfigType which are received from the DlData\_PdschSendReq message (See ([11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).\*\*

For the details, please refer to ([11416051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e-ScP_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and ([11417721](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e-Wtr_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The supported technology types in L1 DL **are shown in table below:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Technology** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor BB** | **Odin BB** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 23R1 onwards: Supported | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 23R1 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-IA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] |
| **FR2-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | - | - | - |
| **FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported | - |

---

### 4.3.5.2 DMRS configuration

#### 4.3.5.2.0-1 DMRS configuration For Loner (ID: 11430709)

**4.3.5.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11430716`)

DMRS configuration
**Loner L1 HW:**

To identify the DMRS configuration for a PDSCH transmission, the 5G-L1-DL uses the following parameters received in the *DlData\_PdschSendReq message (*pdschDmrsConfig structure) :\*\*

* *dmrsScramblingSequenceInt: the value of PDSCH scrambling. It could be set to* physical-layer cell ID and other values.\*\*
* *dlDmrsConfigType : The DMRS configuration type, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211*

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Configuration type** | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **1** | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| **2** | - | - | - |

• *dlDmrsLen: The number of OFDM symbols used for the front-loaded DM-RS, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211.*

Supported value is "1".

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **DMRS length** | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **1 (single-symbol)** | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| **2 (double-symbol)** | - | - | - |

* *dlDmrsMappingType: The PDSCH mapping type, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211*

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Mapping type** | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **Type A** | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| **Type B** | - | - | - |

• *dlDmrsTypeAPos : The position of the front-loaded DMRS for the PDSCH mapping type A, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211*

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Type A position** | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **2** | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| **3** | Supported | Supported | Supported |

• *dlDmrsAddPos : The maximum number of additional DMRS on top of the front-loaded DMRS, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211*

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Additional position** | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **0** | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| **1** | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| **2** | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| **3** | - | - | - |

* *numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData : The number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data, see the index Tables 7.3.1.2.2-1/2/3/4 in 38.212,where the values 1, 2, and 3 refers to CDM groups {0}, {0,1}, and {0, 1,2} respectively.*

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **1** | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| **2** | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| **3** | - | - | - |

**Note: The update in 5GC001037-A is supported in 5GC001037-B from L2-PS perspective.**

* *offsetRbDmrs : Offset to compute the DMRS reference signal*.\*\*

The usage and the value setting of this field please refer to 5G\_L1\_3654\_replaced([11430544](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_DRPfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

* nscId : nSCID as specified in 38.211 §7.4.1.1.1.

Value "0" is supported.

Values {0, 1} are supported.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **0** | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| **1** | Supported | **-** | - |

---

#### 4.3.5.2.0-2 DMRS configuration For Loki (ID: 11430727)

**4.3.5.2.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11430771`)

DMRS configuration
To identify the DMRS configuration for a PDSCH transmission, the 5G-L1-DL uses the following parameters received in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message (pdschDmrsConfig structure) :

* dmrsScramblingSequenceInt: the value of PDSCH scrambling. It could be set to physical-layer cell ID and other values.
* dlDmrsConfigType: The DMRS configuration type, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Configuration type | Configuration type 1 | Configuration type 2 |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | Supported | - |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported | [CB008830-A] Supported [End CB008830-A] |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | [CB008830-A] Supported [End CB008830-A] |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | - |

• dlDmrsLen: The number of OFDM symbols used for the front-loaded DM-RS, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| DMRS length | single-symbol | double-symbol |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | Supported | - |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported | - |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | - |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | - |

* dlDmrsMappingType: The PDSCH mapping type, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Mapping type | Type A | Type B |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | Supported | - |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported | - |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | - |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | - |

• dlDmrsTypeAPos: The position of the front-loaded DMRS for the PDSCH mapping type A, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Type A position | 2 | 3 |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported |

• dlDmrsAddPos: The maximum number of additional DMRS on top of the front-loaded DMRS, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Additional position | 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | Supported | Supported | Supported | - |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported | Supported | - |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported | Supported | - |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported | Supported | - |

* numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData: The number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data, see the index Tables 7.3.1.2.2-1/2/3/4 in 38.212,where the values 1, 2, and 3 refers to CDM groups {0}, {0,1}, and {0, 1,2} respectively.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| The number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data | 1 | 2 | 3 |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | Supported | Supported | - |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported | [CB008830-A] Supported [End CB008830-A] |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported | [CB008830-A] Supported [End CB008830-A] |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported | - |

Note:

[Before CB008830-A]

numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData = 1 or 2 are supported when dlDmrsAddPos = 0, 1, 2.

[CB008830-A]

numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData = {1, 2, 3} are supported when dlDmrsAddPos = 0, 1, 2.

numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData = 3 is supported when dlDmrsConfigType = 2.

[End CB008830-A]

* offsetRbDmrs: Offset to compute the DMRS reference signal.

The usage and the value setting of this field please refer to 5G\_L1\_3654\_replaced([11430544](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_DRPfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

* nscId: nSCID as specified in 38.211 §7.4.1.1.1.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| nscId | 0 | 1 |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | Supported | - |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | - |

#### 4.3.5.2.0-4 DMRS configuration For Odin BB (ID: 12715000)

**4.3.5.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12715001`)

DMRS configuration
To identify the DMRS configuration for a PDSCH transmission, the 5G-L1-DL uses the following parameters received in the *DlData\_PdschSendReq message (*pdschDmrsConfig structure) :\*\*

* *dmrsScramblingSequenceInt: the value of PDSCH scrambling. It could be set to* physical-layer cell ID and other values.\*\*
* *dlDmrsConfigType: The DMRS configuration type, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211*

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Configuration type** **(Odin BB)** | **Configuration type 1** | **Configuration type 2** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** | - |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | [CB012115-SR-IA] Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] | - |

• *dlDmrsLen: The number of OFDM symbols used for the front-loaded DM-RS, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211.*

Supported value is "1".

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **DMRS length** **(Odin BB)** | **single-symbol** | **double-symbol** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** | - |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | [CB012115-SR-IA] Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] | **-** |

* *dlDmrsMappingType: The PDSCH mapping type, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211*

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Mapping type** **(Odin BB)** | **Type A** | **Type B** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** | - |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | [CB012115-SR-IA] Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] | **-** |

• *dlDmrsTypeAPos: The position of the front-loaded DMRS for the PDSCH mapping type A, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211*

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Type A position** **(Odin BB)** | **2** | **3** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | [CB012115-SR-IA] Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] | [CB012115-SR-IA] Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] |

• *dlDmrsAddPos: The maximum number of additional DMRS on top of the front-loaded DMRS, see section 7.4.1.1.1 in 38.211*

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Additional position** **(Odin BB)** | **0** | **1** | **2** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | [CB012115-SR-IA] Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] | [CB012115-SR-IA] Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] | [CB012115-SR-IA] Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] |

* *numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData: The number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data, see the index Tables 7.3.1.2.2-1/2/3/4 in 38.212,where the values 1, 2, and 3 refers to CDM groups {0}, {0,1}, and {0, 1,2} respectively.*

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **The number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data** **(Odin BB)** | **1** | **2** | **3** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | - | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** | - |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | - | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | - | [CB012115-SR-IA] Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] | - |

* *offsetRbDmrs: Offset to compute the DMRS reference signal*.\*\*

The usage and the value setting of this field please refer to 5G\_L1\_3654\_replaced([11430544](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_DRPfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **offsetRbDmrs** **(Odin BB)** | **0** | **other non-zero values** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | [CB012115-SR-IA] Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] | [CB012115-SR-IA] Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] |

* *nscId: nSCID as specified in 38.211 §7.4.1.1.1*.\*\*

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **nscId** **(Odin BB)** | **0** | **1** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-JA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-JA]** | - |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | **[CB012115-SR-LA]** Supported **[End CB012115-SR-LA]** | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | [CB012115-SR-IA] Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] | - |

---

#### 4.3.5.2.0-5 DMRS configuration Determine the DMRS position (ID: 11430831)

**4.3.5.2.0-5.0-4**  (ID: `12717609`)

DMRS configuration
In order to determine the DMRS position(s) (either front-load DMRS(s) or additional DMRS(s)), 5G-L1-DL shall follow the procedure below, where the parameters *dlDmrsMappingType,* dlDmrsAddPos and *dlDmrsLen are got from* DlData\_PdschSendReq message.\*\*\*\*

**Step1: Determine the position of front-load DMRS**

**Step1-1: Position of the 1st DMRS l0 in 38.211 section 7.4.1.1.2**

If (*dlDmrsMappingType == TypeA)*

Then

l0 is equal to *dlDmrsTypeAPos*

Else If (*dlDmrsMappingType == TypeB)*

l0 is equal to 0 as stated in paragraph 7.4.1.1.2 of 38.211

End If

**Step1-2: Position of the 2nd FL DMRS (Applicable only when *dlDmrsLen equals to 2)***

The 2nd DMRS position is set to l0 +1 (see l’ parameter in 38.211 Table 7.4.1.1.2-5)

**Step2: Determine the position of additional DMRS(s)**

Applicable only when *dlDmrsAddPos > 0*

**Step2-1: Determine the right 3GPP Table in 38.211 which specifies the additional DMRS position**

If *dlDmrsLen = 1 (single FL DMRS case)*

5G-L1-DL shall use Table 7.4.1.1.2-3

Else If *dlDmrsLen = 2 (two FL DMRS case)*

5G-L1-DL shall use Table 7.4.1.1.2-4

End If

**Step2-2: Determine the position of the additional DMRS(s) using the right Table determined in Step2-1**

If (*dlDmrsMappingType == TypeA)*

Then

5G-L1-DL shall calculate the "PDSCH duration in symbols" (1st column in Tables 7.4.1.1.2-3 and 7.4.1.1.2-4) as *startSymbol +* numOfPdschSymbols.\*\*

Else If (*dlDmrsMappingType == TypeB)*

5G-L1-DL shall calculate the "PDSCH duration in symbols" as *numOfPdschSymbols*

End If

**Note: the additional DMRS symbol position is in symbol l1 = 12 instead of 11 with the following configuration:**

* dlDmrsLen = 1
* dlDmrsAddPos = 1,
* lteCrsOption ([11436249](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_PeevvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):
* 1, 3, 4, 5, 6 for symmetric FDD DSS
* 1, 3, 4, 5 for asymmetric FDD DSS.

5G-L1-DL shall use *dlDmrsAddPos and the computed "PDSCH duration in symbols" to determine the position of the additional DMRSs (could be either 1 or 2) using the selected Table in Step2-1.*

The supported technology types in L1 DL **are shown in table below:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Technology** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor BB** | **Odin BB** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 23R1 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-JA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-JA] |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-LA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-LA] |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: **Supported** | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 23R1 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-IA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] |
| **FR2-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | - | - | - |
| **FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported | - |

---

DMRS antenna port and precoding
DMRS used antenna ports are the same as the ones used for PDSCH data transmission as described in 5G\_L1\_9242\_replaced([11432235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_Hio_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The precoding operation for DL DMRS in each subcell is depicted in following figure.

* Precoding for DL DMRS up to rank 2 with 2 Tx transmission

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a signal processing flow for a wireless communication system. The diagram shows two parallel processing paths, each starting with input signals labeled "r(0), r(1), ..." and associated with "Logical antenna port 0" and "Logical antenna port 1" respectively.
Both input paths converge into a block labeled "Precoding for DMRS (same precoding matrix as PDSCH)". This block outputs two signals, "DMRS precoding output\_0" and "DMRS precoding output\_1", which then feed into separate "RE Mapping" blocks.
Each "RE Mapping" block's output is directed to a final block representing a physical antenna port. The top path leads to "Physical antenna port 0 Polarization +45° of the X-Pol Beam (AxC0/eAxC0)", and the bottom path leads to "Physical antenna port 1 Polarization -45° of the X-Pol Beam (AxC1/eAxC1)".
The diagram uses blue arrows to indicate the flow of signals between the blocks. The overall structure suggests a process of precoding and mapping of Demodulation Reference Signals (DMRS) to specific physical antenna ports with defined polarizations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4044.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The precoding of DMRS depends on the precoding matrix W and the Orthogonal Cover Code (OCC) terms denoted as OCC\_factor in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_302\_replaced([11096689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmzqFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Precoding matrix W for DMRS:

* For DL CL 2 Tx transmission rank 1 and 2, the precoding matrix used for DMRS is the same one used for precoding of PDSCH data and specified in 5G\_L1\_2075\_replaced([11434237](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_L0GPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* For DL OL 2 Tx transmission rank 1 and 2, the precoding matrix used for DMRS is the same one used for precoding of PDSCH data and specified in 5G\_L1\_5060\_replaced([11433389](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_J-5_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The OCC\_factor can be derived from Table 7.4.1.1.2-1 **and Table 7.4.1.1.2-2 in 3GPP TS 38.211 § 7.4.1.1.2 according to the parity of the DMRS sequence index indicated by k' (could be 0 or 1) and index l' *of the DMRS symbol (l'=0 for 1 FL DMRS symbol and l'=0 and 1 for 2 FL DMRS symbols as indicated in Table 7.4.1.1.2-5 in 38.211). The OCC\_factor is the product of [image-1][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4044.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] and[image-2][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4044.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]both provided by Table 7.4.1.1.2-1 in 38.211 for DMRS configuration type 1 and Table 7.4.1.1.2-2 for DMRS configuration type 2 . The term [image-3][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4044.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] can be seen as FD OCC and [image-4][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4044.003.png[/image\_path][/image-4] is TD OCC.***

 For logical antenna ports in {1000, 1001, 1002, 1003, 1004, 1005}*[image-5][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4044.005.png[/image\_path][/image-5] is always 1 and OCC\_factor becomes simply [image-6][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4044.006.png[/image\_path][/image-6] so OCC\_factor* depends only on k'.\*\*

The RE mapping for DMRS configuration type 1 and 2 **depends on the parity of the DMRS sequence index as follows (k denotes the RE or sub-carrier number):**

 RE mapping of even index of DMRS sequence (k'=0):

For DMRS configuration type 1,

o on k=4m when logical antenna port = 1000, 1001.

o on k=4m+1 when logical antenna port = 1002, 1003**.**

**For DMRS configuration type 2,**

o on k=6m when logical antenna port = 1000, 1001**.**

o on k=6m+2 when logical antenna port = 1002, 1003**.**

o on k=6m+4 when logical antenna port = 1004, 1005**.**

 RE mapping of odd index of DMRS sequence (k'=1):

For DMRS configuration type 1,

o on k=4m+2 when logical antenna port = 1000, 1001.

o on k=4m+3 when logical antenna port = 1002, 1003.

**For DMRS configuration type 2,**

o on k=6m + 1 when logical antenna port = 1000, 1001**.**

o on k=6m+3 when logical antenna port = 1002, 1003**.**

o on k=6m+5 when logical antenna port = 1004, 1005**.**

The outputs of the DMRS precoding summarized in following tables are for *l' =0.*

For DMRS configuration type 1

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **2 Tx transmission rank 1** |  | **AP0= 1000** | **AP0= 1001** | **AP0= 1002** | **AP0= 1003** |
| DMRS precoding output \_0 (k) | k=4m | w00. r(2m) | w00. r(2m) | 0 | 0 |
| k=4m+1 | 0 | 0 | w00. r(2m) | w00. r(2m) |  |
| k=4m+2 | w00. r(2m+1) | -w00. r(2m+1) | 0 | 0 |  |
| k=4m+3 | 0 | 0 | w00. r(2m+1) | -w00. r(2m+1) |  |
| DMRS precoding output \_1 (k) | k=4m | w10. r(2m) | w10. r(2m) | 0 | 0 |
| k=4m+1 | 0 | 0 | w10. r(2m) | w10. r(2m) |  |
| k=4m+2 | w10. r(2m+1) | -w10. r(2m+1) | 0 | 0 |  |
| k=4m+3 | 0 | 0 | w10. r(2m+1) | -w10. r(2m+1) |  |

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2 Tx transmission rank 2 |  | AP0, AP1=1000, 1002 | AP0, AP1=1000, 1001 | AP0, AP1=1002, 1003 |
| DMRS precoding output \_0 (k) | k=4m | w00. r(2m) | (w00 + w01). r(2m) | 0 |
| k=4m+1 | w01. r(2m) | 0 | (w00 + w01). r(2m) |  |
| k=4m+2 | w00. r(2m+1) | (w00 - w01). r(2m+1) | 0 |  |
| k=4m+3 | w01. r(2m+1) | 0 | (w00 - w01). r(2m+1) |  |
| DMRS precoding output \_1 (k) | k=4m | w10. r(2m) | (w10 + w11). r(2m) | 0 |
| k=4m+1 | w11. r(2m) | 0 | (w10 + w11). r(2m) |  |
| k=4m+2 | w10. r(2m+1) | (w10 – w11). r(2m+1) | 0 |  |
| k=4m+3 | w11. r(2m+1) | 0 | (w10 – w11). r(2m+1) |  |

For DMRS configuration type 2

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **2 Tx transmission rank 1** |  | **AP0= 1000** | **AP0= 1001** | **AP0= 1002** | **AP0= 1003** | **AP0= 1004** | **AP0= 1005** |
| DMRS precoding output \_0 (k) | k=6m | w00. r(2m) | w00. r(2m) | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| k=6m+1 | w00. r(2m+1) | -w00. r(2m+1) | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |  |
| k=6m+2 | 0 | 0 | w00. r(2m) | w00. r(2m) | 0 | 0 |  |
| k=6m+3 | 0 | 0 | w00. r(2m+1) | -w00. r(2m+1) | 0 | 0 |  |
| k=6m+4 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | w00. r(2m) | w00. r(2m) |  |
| k=6m+5 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | w00. r(2m+1) | -w00. r(2m+1) |  |
| DMRS precoding output \_1 (k) | k=6m | w10. r(2m) | w10. r(2m) | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| k=6m+1 | w10. r(2m+1) | -w10. r(2m+1) | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |  |
| k=6m+2 | 0 | 0 | w10. r(2m) | w10. r(2m) | 0 | 0 |  |
| k=6m+3 | 0 | 0 | w10. r(2m+1) | -w10. r(2m+1) | 0 | 0 |  |
| k=6m+4 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | w10. r(2m) | w10. r(2m) |  |
| k=6m+5 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | w10. r(2m+1) | -w10. r(2m+1) |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |  |  |
| **2 Tx transmission rank 2** |  | **AP0, AP1=1000, 1002** | **AP0, AP1=1000, 1001** | **AP0, AP1=1002, 1003** | **AP0, AP1=1004, 1005** |  |  |
| DMRS precoding output \_0 (k) | k=6m | w00. r(2m) | (w00 + w01). r(2m) | 0 | 0 |  |  |
| k=6m+1 | w00. r(2m+1) | (w00 - w01). r(2m+1) | 0 | 0 |  |  |  |
| k=6m+2 | w01. r(2m) | 0 | (w00 + w01). r(2m) | 0 |  |  |  |
| k=6m+3 | w01. r(2m+1) | 0 | (w00 - w01). r(2m+1) | 0 |  |  |  |
| k=6m+4 | 0 | 0 | 0 | (w00 + w01). r(2m) |  |  |  |
| k=6m+5 | 0 | 0 | 0 | (w00 - w01). r(2m+1) |  |  |  |
| DMRS precoding output \_1 (k) | k=6m | w10. r(2m) | (w10 + w11). r(2m) | 0 | 0 |  |  |
| k=6m+1 | w10. r(2m+1) | (w10 – w11). r(2m+1) | 0 | 0 |  |  |  |
| k=6m+2 | w11. r(2m) | 0 | (w10 + w11). r(2m) | 0 |  |  |  |
| k=6m+3 | w11. r(2m+1) | 0 | (w10 – w11). r(2m+1) | 0 |  |  |  |
| k=6m+4 | 0 | 0 | 0 | (w10 + w11). r(2m) |  |  |  |
| k=6m+5 | 0 | 0 | 0 | (w10 – w11). r(2m+1) |  |  |  |

The supported technology types in L1 DL **are shown in table below:**

For DMRS configuration type 1**:**

| **FR1-FDD-eCPRI-7-2a** | - | Supported | Supported | [CB012115-SR-LA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-LA] |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | - | Supported | Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-eCPRI-7-2a** | Supported | Supported | Supported | [CB012115-SR-IA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] |
| **FR2-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | - | - | - |
| **FR2-TDD-eCPRI-7-2a** | Supported | Supported | Supported | - |

For DMRS configuration type 2

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

DMRS used antenna ports are the same as the ones used for PDSCH data transmission as described in 5G\_L1\_9242\_replaced([11432235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_Hio_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The precoding operation for DL DMRS in each subcell is depicted in following figure.

* Precoding for DL DMRS up to rank 4 with 4 Tx transmission

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a signal processing flow for Demodulation Reference Signal (DMRS) precoding in a communication system.
The diagram shows four logical antenna ports (0, 1, 2, and 3) as inputs, each receiving signals denoted as "r(0), r(1), ...". These inputs are fed into a central block labeled "Precoding for DMRS (same precoding matrix as PDSCH)".
The output of this precoding block is split into four "DMRS precoding output" streams, labeled 0 through 3. Each of these streams then goes into a separate "RE Mapping" block.
The outputs of the "RE Mapping" blocks are then directed to four different "Physical antenna port" destinations. These physical antenna ports are described with their polarization and the specific X-Pol Beam they are associated with:
\* **Physical antenna port 0:** Polarization +45° or -45° of the 1st X-Pol Beam (AxC0/eAxC0/SxCO)
\* **Physical antenna port 1:** Polarization -45° or +45° of the 1st X-Pol Beam (AxC1/eAxC1/SxC1)
\* **Physical antenna port 2:** Polarization +45° or -45° of the 2nd X-Pol Beam (AxC2/eAxC2/SxC2)
\* **Physical antenna port 3:** Polarization -45° or +45° of the 2nd X-Pol Beam (AxC3/eAxC3/SxC3)
There are cross-connections shown between the outputs of the RE Mapping blocks and the physical antenna ports. Specifically, the output of "DMRS precoding output\_0" maps to "Physical antenna port 0". The output of "DMRS precoding output\_1" maps to "Physical antenna port 1". The output of "DMRS precoding output\_2" maps to "Physical antenna port 2". The output of "DMRS precoding output\_3" maps to "Physical antenna port 3".
The diagram uses blue arrows to indicate the flow of signals between the blocks.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4046.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The precoding of DMRS depends on the precoding matrix W and the Orthogonal Cover Code (OCC) terms denoted as OCC\_factor in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_302\_replaced([11096689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmzqFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Precoding matrix W for DMRS:

* For DL OL 4x4 MIMO rank 1 to 4, the precoding matrix used for DMRS is the same one used for precoding of PDSCH data and specified in 5G\_L1\_5098\_replaced([11433633](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_Kl8_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* For DL CL 4x4 MIMO rank 1 to 4, the precoding matrix used for DMRS is the same one used for precoding of PDSCH data and specified in 5G\_L1\_5103\_replaced([11434463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_MbJPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* For DL CL 8x4 MIMO rank 1 to 4, the precoding matrix used for DMRS is the same one used for precoding of PDSCH data and specified in 5G\_L1\_5109\_replaced([11435279](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_NpRPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The OCC\_factor can be derived from Table 7.4.1.1.2-1 and Table 7.4.1.1.2-2 in 3GPP TS 38.211 § 7.4.1.1.2 according to the parity of the DMRS sequence index indicated by k' (could be 0 or 1) and index l' *of the DMRS symbol (l'=0 for 1 FL DMRS symbol and l'=0 and 1 for 2 FL DMRS symbols as indicated in Table 7.4.1.1.2-5 in 38.211). The OCC\_factor is the product of [image-1][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4046.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] and[image-2][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4046.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]both provided by Table 7.4.1.1.2-1 in 38.211 for DMRS configuration type 1 and Table 7.4.1.1.2-2 for DMRS configuration type 2 . The term [image-3][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4046.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] can be seen as FD OCC and [image-4][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4046.003.png[/image\_path][/image-4] is TD OCC.*

 For logical antenna ports in {1000, 1001, 1002, 1003}*[image-5][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4046.005.png[/image\_path][/image-5] is always 1 and OCC\_factor becomes simply [image-6][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4046.006.png[/image\_path][/image-6] so OCC\_factor* depends only on k'.\*\*

 For logical antenna ports in {1004, 1005, 1006, 1007}  [image-7][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4046.007.png[/image\_path][/image-7] is 1 for l'=0 and -1 for l'=1. Therefore, the OCC\_factor becomes simply [image-8][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4046.008.png[/image\_path][/image-8] for l'=0 and [image-9][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4046.009.png[/image\_path][/image-9] for l'=1.

The RE mapping for DMRS configuration type 1 and 2**depends on the parity of the DMRS sequence index as follows (k denotes the RE or sub-carrier number):**

 RE mapping of even index of DMRS sequence (k'=0):

For DMRS configuration type 1,

o on k=4m when logical antenna port = 1000, 1001~~, 1004 or 1005~~.

o on k=4m+1 when logical antenna port = 1002, 1003~~, 1006 or 1007~~**.**

**For DMRS configuration type 2,**

o on k=6m when logical antenna port = 1000, 1001**.**

o on k=6m+2 when logical antenna port = 1002, 1003**.**

o on k=6m+4 when logical antenna port = 1004, 1005**.**

 RE mapping of odd index of DMRS sequence (k'=1):

For DMRS configuration type 1,

o on k=4m+2 when logical antenna port = 1000, 1001~~, 1004 or 1005~~.

o on k=4m+3 when logical antenna port = 1002, 1003~~, 1006 or 1007~~.

**For DMRS configuration type 2,**

o on k=6m + 1 when logical antenna port = 1000, 1001**.**

o on k=6m+3 when logical antenna port = 1002, 1003**.**

o on k=6m+5 when logical antenna port = 1004, 1005**.**

| k=4m+2 | w00. r(2m+1) | (w00 - w01). r(2m+1) | 0 |  |  |

### 4.3.5.4 Requirements for testing DMRS

#### 4.3.5.4.0-1 Requirements for testing DMRS DSS (ID: 11431405)

**4.3.5.4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11431425`)

Requirements for testing DMRS
L1 shall support additional DMRS symbol position l1 = 12 instead of 11 with the following configuration for FR1 FDD bandwidth 10/15/20 MHz:

* dlDmrsLen = 1
* dlDmrsAddPos = 1
* lteCrsOption:

[CB006895-A] parity from 5GC001586

* 1, 3

[End CB006895-A]

[5GC001745-C]

* 4, 5, 6

[End 5GC001745-C]

[RP003187-L] 5G-L1-DL shall also support requirements above with lteCrsOption={1,3} (parity from 5GC001586), and lteCrsOption={4,5,6} (parity from 5GC001745) in Thor for FR1 FDD (DSS) CPRI . [End RP003187-L]

[CB009273-A] 5G-L1-DL should also support lteCrsOption={1,3}(parity from 5GC001586) and lteCrsOption={ 4,5,6}(parity from 5GC001745)  on ASOE for FR1 FDD DSS. [End CB009273-A]

---

#### 4.3.5.4.0-2 Requirements for testing DMRS DMRS type 1 (ID: 11431435)

**4.3.5.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11431444`)

Requirements for testing DMRS
L1 shall support FDM of PDSCH data and DMRS (DlData\_PdschSendReq::numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData = 1, up to 2 layers by L2 with:

- PDSCH DMRS type 1

- PDSCH mapping type A

- single symbol length DMRS

- 0 or 1 additional DMRS for data PDSCH scheduled by DCI 1\_1

- dmrs-TypeA-Position equals 2 or 3

- PTRS on or off

- FDD / TDD FR1, FR2

[CB007814-G] 5G-L1-DL shall also support requirements above in ABIO for FR1 FDD eCPRI. [End CB007814-G]

[CB009784-A] DL PTRS functionality is disabled for FDD and FR1 TDD from 23R2 in Loner and Loki. [End CB009784-A]

---

#### 4.3.5.4.0-3 Requirements for testing DMRS DMRS type 2 (ID: 11431453)

**4.3.5.4.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11431463`)

Requirements for testing DMRS
L1 shall support DMRS transmission associated with PDSCH with following configurations for TDD FR1

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PDSCH configuration | Loki |  |  |  | Thor |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | SRAN |  |  |  | SRAN |  |  |  | RINLINE2 |  |
|  | TDD-.2a-eCPRI |  | TDD-FR1-CPRI |  | TDD-.2a-eCPRI |  | TDD-FR1-CPRI |  | TDD-.2a-eCPRI |  |
| dlDmrsConfigType | 2 |  | 2 |  | 2 |  | 2 |  | 2 |  |
| dlDmrsLen | 1(single FL DMRS) |  | 1(single FL DMRS) |  | 1(single FL DMRS) |  | 1(single FL DMRS) |  | 1(single FL DMRS) |  |
| dlDmrsMappingType | TypeA |  | TypeA |  | TypeA |  | TypeA |  | TypeA |  |
| dlDmrsAddPos | {0,1,2} |  | {0,1,2} |  | {0,1,2} |  | {0,1,2} |  | {0,1,2} |  |
| dlDmrsTypeAPos | {2,3} |  | {2,3} |  | {2,3} |  | {2,3} |  | {2,3} |  |
| numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData | Rank 1 or 2 | Rank 3 or 4 | Rank 1 or 2 | Rank 3 or 4 | Rank 1 or 2 | Rank 3 or 4 | Rank 1 or 2 | Rank 3 or 4 | Rank 1 or 2 | Rank 3 or 4 |
| {3,2,1} | {3,2} | {3,2,1} | {3,2} | {3,2,1} | {3,2} | {3,2,1} | {3,2} | {3,2,1} | {3,2} |  |
| nscId | 0,1 |  | 0 |  | 0 |  | 0 |  | 0,1 |  |
| offsetRbDmrs | >=0 |  | >= 0 |  | >= 0 |  | >= 0 |  | >= 0 |  |

* Note:  The number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data, see the index Tables 7.3.1.2.2-1/2/3/4 in 38.212,where the values 1, 2, and 3 refers to CDM groups {0}, {0,1}, and {0, 1,2} respectively.

---

## 4.3.6 PDSCH PTRS

### 4.3.6.1 Overview

**4.3.6.1.0-3**  (ID: `12717625`)

Overview
PTRS supported on different L1 hardware platforms:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Technology | Loner | Loki | Thor BB | Odin BB |
| FR1 TDD | PTRS OFF | PTRS OFF | PTRS OFF | [CB012115-SR-IA]: PTRS OFF [End CB012115-SR-IA] |
| FR1 FDD | PTRS OFF | PTRS OFF | PTRS OFF | [CB012115-SR-JA]: PTRS OFF [End CB012115-SR-JA] |
| FR2 TDD | PTRS OFF PTRS ON | PTRS OFF PTRS ON | PTRS OFF PTRS ON | - |

Note: No UE has supported DL PTRS for FR1 and no chip vendor has plan to support this so in live network PTRS ON scenario never happens for FR1 from system view. This indicates that for FR1 testing with PTRS ON is not needed for release before and from 23R2.

---

### 4.3.6.2 PTRS configuration

#### 4.3.6.2.0-1 PTRS configuration For Loner (ID: 11431519)

**4.3.6.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11431528`)

PTRS configuration
**Loner L1 HW:**

DL PTRS generation is configured in *DlPtrsConfig in* DlData\_PdschSendReq message.\*\*

* *dlPtrsFlag:  Indicates whether DL PTRS are enabled or not.*

For both FR1 and FR2, both value '0' and value '1' are supported for PDSCH (except for Msg2/Msg4), with 0 or 1 additional DMRS. For Msg2/Msg4, only value '0' is supported, i.e. no PTRS is transmitted in Msg2/Msg4. Instead, 2 additional DMRS shall be transmitted.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **dlPtrsFlag** | Enabled (1), Disabled (0) | Enabled (1), Disabled (0) | Enabled (1), Disabled (0) |

When DL PTRS is disabled (i.e. IE *dlPtrsFlag set to 0), parameters below are meaningless.*

* *dlPtrsTimeDensity: specifies the value for LPTRS from 38.211 § 7.4.1.2.2*

When PTRS is present (i.e. IE dlPtrsFlag = 1):

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **dlPtrsTimeDensity** | 1 | 1, 4 (Comformance test) | 1 |

* *dlPtrsFrequencyDensity: specifies the value for KPTRS from 3GPP TS 38.211 § 7.4.1.2.2.*

When PTRS is present (i.e. IE dlPtrsFlag = 1):

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **dlPtrsFrequencyDensity** | 2, 4 | 2, 4 | 2, 4 |

* *dlPtrsNumOfPorts: specifies the number of antenna ports for PTRS transmission.*

Only one single logical antenna port is used for DL PT-RS. IE *dlPtrsNumOfPorts is set to 1. 5G-L1-DL shall determine the PT-RS antenna port index according to the number of codeword(s) and the DMRS antenna port(s) as specified in 38.214 section 5.1.6.3.*

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **dlPtrsNumOfPorts** | 1 | 1 | 1 |

5G-L1-DL shall use the IE *antPort in* DlData\_PdschSendReq message to derive the DMRS antenna port(s). For the supported codeword configurations, please refer to the SoC statement for 38.211 section 7.3.1.3 which is associated to the appropriate system release, 5G\_UP\_IF\_596.\*\*

Depending on the precoding, it could still be mapped to several physical antenna ports. Note that in the specific case of SM2 precoding with the identity matrix, PTRS is sent on a single physical antenna port.

When one codeword is scheduled, the PTRS antenna port is associated with the lowest indexed DMRS antenna port among the DM-RS antenna ports assigned for the PDSCH as specified in 38.214 section 5.1.6.3.

* *dlPtrsResElemOffset: specifies the value for DL-PTRS-RE-offset from 38.211 §7.4.1.2.2*

When PTRS is present (i.e. IE dlPtrsFlag = 1):

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **dlPtrsResElemOffset** | 0 | 0 | 0 |

**[CB009784-A] DL PTRS functionality is disabled for FDD and FR1 TDD from 23R2 in Loner and Loki.** [End CB009784-A]\*\*\*\*

---

#### 4.3.6.2.0-2 PTRS configuration For Loki (ID: 11431538)

**4.3.6.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11431552`)

PTRS configuration
Loki L1 HW:

DL PT-RS generation is configured in DlPtrsConfig in DlData\_PdschSendReq message.

* dlPtrsFlag: Indicates whether DL PTRS are enabled or not.

Indicates whether DL PTRS are enabled or not. When DL PTRS is disabled, PTRS resources shall be used for PDSCH data. For Msg2/Msg4, only value '0' is used, i.e. no PTRS is transmitted in Msg2/Msg4. Instead, 3 DMRS shall be transmitted.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | TDD FR1 | TDD FR2 | FDD |
| dlPtrsFlag | Enabled (1) Disabled (0) | [CB007596-A] Enabled (1) Disabled (0) [End CB007596-A] | Enabled (1) Disabled (0) |

When DL PTRS is disabled (i.e. IE dlPtrsFlag set to 0), parameters below are meaningless.

* dlPtrsTimeDensity: specifies the value for LPTRS from 38.211 § 7.4.1.2.2

When PTRS is present (i.e. IE dlPtrsFlag = 1):

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | TDD FR1 | TDD FR2 | FDD |
| dlPtrsTimeDensity | 1 | [CB007596-A] 1 [End CB007596-A] [CB007403-D] For conformance tests: 1, 4 [End CB007403-D] | 1 |

* dlPtrsFrequencyDensity: specifies the value for KPTRS from 3GPP TS 38.211 § 7.4.1.2.2.

When PTRS is present (i.e. IE dlPtrsFlag = 1):

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | TDD FR1 | TDD FR2 | FDD |
| dlPtrsFrequencyDensity | 2, 4 | [CB007596-A] 2, 4 [End CB007596-A] | 2, 4 |

* dlPtrsNumOfPorts: specifies the number of antenna ports for PTRS transmission

Only one single logical antenna port is used for DL PT-RS. IE dlPtrsNumOfPorts is set to 1. 5G-L1-DL shall determine the PT-RS antenna port index according to the number of codeword(s) and the DMRS antenna port(s) as specified in 38.214 section 5.1.6.3.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | TDD FR1 | TDD FR2 | FDD |
| dlPtrsNumOfPorts | 1 | [CB007596-A] 1 [End CB007596-A] | 1 |

5G-L1-DL shall use the IE antPort in DlData\_PdschSendReq message to derive the DMRS antenna port(s). For the supported codeword configurations, please refer to the SoC statement for 38.211 section 7.3.1.3 which is associated to the appropriate system release, 5G\_UP\_IF\_596.

Depending on the precoding, it could still be mapped to several physical antenna ports. Note that in the specific case of SM2 precoding with the identity matrix, PTRS is sent on a single physical antenna port.

When one codeword is scheduled, the PT-RS antenna port is associated with the lowest indexed DMRS antenna port among the DM-RS antenna ports assigned for the PDSCH as specified in 38.214 section 5.1.6.3.

* dlPtrsResElemOffset: specifies the value for DL-PTRS-RE-offset from 38.211 §7.4.1.2.2

When PTRS is present (i.e. IE dlPtrsFlag = 1):

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | TDD FR1 | TDD FR2 | FDD |
| dlPtrsResElemOffset | 0 | [CB007596-A] 0 [End CB007596-A] | 0 |

[CB007814-A] 5G-L1-DL shall also support requirement above in ABIO for FR1 FDD eCPRI. [End CB007814-A]

[CB009784-A] DL PTRS functionality is disabled for FDD and FR1 TDD from 23R2 in Loner and Loki. [End CB009784-A]

---

#### 4.3.6.2.0-3 PTRS configuration For Thor (ID: 11431563)

**4.3.6.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11431572`)

PTRS configuration
**Thor L1 HW:**

DL PT-RS generation is configured in *DlPtrsConfig in* DlData\_PdschSendReq message.\*\*

* *dlPtrsFlag: Indicates whether DL PTRS are enabled or not.*

Indicates whether DL PTRS are enabled or not. When DL PTRS is disabled, PTRS resources shall be used for PDSCH data. For Msg2/Msg4, only value '0' is used, i.e. no PTRS is transmitted in Msg2/Msg4. Instead, 3 DMRS shall be transmitted.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **dlPtrsFlag** | Disabled (0) | **[CB010576-A]** Enabled (1) Disabled (0) **[End CB010576-A]** | Disabled (0) |

When DL PTRS is disabled (i.e. IE *dlPtrsFlag set to 0), parameters below are meaningless.*

* *dlPtrsTimeDensity: specifies the value for LPTRS from 38.211 § 7.4.1.2.2*

When PTRS is present (i.e. IE dlPtrsFlag = 1):

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **dlPtrsTimeDensity** | N.A. | **[CB010576-A]** 1, 4 Note: 4 is for conformance test only **[End CB010576-A]** | N.A. |

* *dlPtrsFrequencyDensity: specifies the value for KPTRS from 3GPP TS 38.211 § 7.4.1.2.2.*

When PTRS is present (i.e. IE dlPtrsFlag = 1):

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **dlPtrsFrequencyDensity** | N.A. | **[CB010576-A]** 2, 4 **[End CB010576-A]** | N.A. |

* *dlPtrsNumOfPorts: specifies the number of antenna ports for PTRS transmission*

Only one single logical antenna port is used for DL PT-RS. IE *dlPtrsNumOfPorts is set to 1. 5G-L1-DL shall determine the PT-RS antenna port index according to the number of codeword(s) and the DMRS antenna port(s) as specified in 38.214 section 5.1.6.3.*

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **dlPtrsNumOfPorts** | N.A. | **[CB010576-A]** 1 **[End CB010576-A]** | N.A. |

1. 5G-L1-DL shall use the IE *antPort in* DlData\_PdschSendReq message to derive the DMRS antenna port(s). For the supported codeword configurations, please refer to the SoC statement for 38.211 section 7.3.1.3 which is associated to the appropriate system release, 5G\_UP\_IF\_596.\*\*
2. Depending on the precoding, it could still be mapped to several physical antenna ports. Note that in the specific case of SM2 precoding with the identity matrix, PTRS is sent on a single physical antenna port.

When one codeword is scheduled, the PT-RS antenna port is associated with the lowest indexed DMRS antenna port among the DM-RS antenna ports assigned for the PDSCH as specified in 38.214 section 5.1.6.3.

* *dlPtrsResElemOffset: specifies the value for DL-PTRS-RE-offset from 38.211 §7.4.1.2.2*

When PTRS is present (i.e. IE dlPtrsFlag = 1):

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **TDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **FDD** |
| **dlPtrsResElemOffset** | N.A. | **[CB010576-A]** 0 **[End CB010576-A]** | N.A. |

### 4.3.6.3 PTRS antenna port and precoding

#### 4.3.6.3.0-1 PTRS antenna port and precoding PTRS antenna port (ID: 11431594)

**4.3.6.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11431603`)

PTRS antenna port and precoding
PTRS are transmitted on the OFDM symbols and PRBs belonging to the corresponding PDSCH allocation. PTRS can never be transmitted on OFDM symbols which are fully reserved for DMRS transmission.

The number of logical antenna ports used for PTRS is configurable through a higher layer parameter which is conveyed to L1 with the parameter “dlPtrsNumOfPorts” in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message.

Only one single logical antenna port is used for DL PT-RS. IE dlPtrsNumOfPorts is set to 1.

A single logical antenna port is used for PTRS for TDD and FDD i.e. The DL PTRS are transmitted on the first logical antenna port allocated for DL DMRS.

---

11431626:
PTRS antenna port and precoding
PTRS is precoded with the same precoding as PDSCH:

 Although PTRS uses a single logical antenna port, it can be sent through 1 or 2 physical antenna ports for 2x2 MIMO or through 1 to 4 physical antenna ports for 4x4 MIMO in each subcell, depending on the precoding matrix that is used as shown in following figures.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating two different signal processing paths for PTRS (Phase Tracking Reference Signal) sequences in a communication system. Both paths start with an input signal `r(0), r(1)...` and a PTRS sequence directed to a logical antenna port AP0.
The top path shows a single processing branch:
1. **Precoding for PTRS (same precoding matrix as PDSCH):** This block takes the input signal and applies precoding.
2. **RE mapping for logical antenna port AP0:** The precoded signal is then mapped to resource elements for logical antenna port AP0.
3. **Physical antenna port 0 Polarization + 45° of the X-Pol Beam:** The signal is transmitted through physical antenna port 0 with a polarization of +45° of the X-Pol Beam.
4. **Physical antenna port 1 Polarization - 45° of the X-Pol Beam:** Another output from the RE mapping block goes to physical antenna port 1 with a polarization of -45° of the X-Pol Beam.
The bottom path shows a more complex processing with multiple outputs:
1. **Precoding for PTRS (same precoding matrix as PDSCH):** Similar to the top path, this block applies precoding to the input signal.
2. **RE mapping for logical antenna port AP0:** The precoded signals are then mapped to resource elements for logical antenna port AP0. This block receives four precoded signals: `W00 r(0) W00 r(1)...`, `W10 r(0) W10 r(1)...`, `W20 r(0) W20 r(1)...`, and `W30 r(0) W30 r(1)...`.
3. **Physical antenna port 0 Polarization + 45° of the 1st X-Pol Beam (AxC0/eAxC0):** One output from the RE mapping goes to physical antenna port 0.
4. **Physical antenna port 1 Polarization - 45° of the 1st X-Pol Beam (AxC1/eAxC1):** Another output from the RE mapping goes to physical antenna port 1.
5. **Physical antenna port 2 Polarization + 45° of the 2nd X-Pol Beam (AxC2/eAxC2):** A third output from the RE mapping goes to physical antenna port 2.
6. **Physical antenna port 3 Polarization - 45° of the 2nd X-Pol Beam (AxC3/eAxC3):** The final output from the RE mapping goes to physical antenna port 3.
The diagram highlights the difference in how PTRS signals are processed and mapped to physical antenna ports, potentially for different antenna configurations or beamforming strategies. The top path appears to be a simpler 2-port configuration, while the bottom path illustrates a more complex 4-port configuration with cross-connections between the RE mapping and physical antenna ports.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4072.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

 Precoding is such that the PTRS sequence is multiplied by the first column of the precoding matrix W. This gives an array of 2 elements for 2x2 MIMO or 4 elements for 4x4 MIMO, which are sent on each physical antenna port. Note that in the specific case of precoding with the identity matrix, PTRS is sent on a single physical antenna port.

 Precoding, RE and antenna mapping for DL PTRS in each subcell are illustrated in above figures for 2x2 and 4x4 MIMO respectively. Matrix W is the same precoding matrix as the one used for precoding of PDSCH.

| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | - | - |

#### 4.3.7.1.2 PDSCH Transport Block size

**4.3.7.1.2.0-1**  (ID: `11431781`)

PDSCH Transport Block size
The Transport Block Size (TBS) is a function of the used Modulation, Coding Scheme (MCS) and the number of allocated PRBs. It also depends on the number of layers used.

L2 determines the TBS which is compliant to 3GPP TS 38.214 § 5.1.3.2. 3GPP specifies MCS tables. Depending on the selected MCS table the size of the transport blocks is different. 5G-L2-PS computes TBS and sends it to L1 with the parameter “tbSize\_bits” in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message. Details about the TBS determination can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4635\_replaced([11192394](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvkkgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

* The exhaustive set of TBS values that can be configured in 3GPP release 15 is provided in a look up table (implemented by 5G-L2-PS) “lut\_tbs.xls” stored in <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2F5G%20Architecture%20documents%2F5G%20NR%20TBS%20Tables.> This table contains 568 entries (with one TBS value for each entry) which are uniquely identified by an index “TBS\_index”. To find the right TBS value from this table for a given configuration one has to find the corresponding “TBS\_index”.
* Based on the selected MCS, the number of layers, the number of PRBs granted, the selected table (64QAM, 256QAM, 1024QAM or qam64LowSE) and the number of data symbols, the “TBS\_index” can be determined from another look up table “lut\_all\_mcs\_rb\_tbs\_index”.
* For DL BTS Conformance Tests (conformanceTestMode from DlCell\_SetupReq message is set to True), new set of TBS tables should be used and are stored in   <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/5G%20Architecture%20documents/5G%20NR%20TBS%20Tables/tbs_calculator_1037.xlsx?d=wd5e542f5b0af4cbc88687c1ce086a4b2&csf=1&e=NIgDLx.>

In this specification, the number of bits in the TB is denoted by A. The bits in the TB are denoted by a0, a1, …, aA-1.

---

attachment 11431812: PDSCH CRC attachment 11431812: PDSCH CRC attachment 11431812: PDSCH CRC attachment 11431812: PDSCH CRC attachment 11431812: PDSCH CRC attachment Error detection is provided on each TB through a Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC). The entire TB is used to calculate the CRC parity bits. The parity bits are computed and attached to the TB according to 3GPP TS 38.212 § 5.1 and § 7.2.1, by setting LTB to 24 bits and using the generator polynomial [image-0][image_desc]The image contains the text "gCRC24A(D)". The text is in a light color against a white background. The letters are clear and legible.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_4090.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] if A &gt; 3824; and by setting LTB to 16 bits and using the generator polynomial [image-1][image_desc]The image contains the text "g CRC24A(D)". The text is in a light color against a white background. The characters are clear and legible.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_4090.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] otherwise. The bits in the TB after CRC attachment are denoted by b0, b1, …, bB-1, where B = A + LTB with LTB = 24 if A &gt; 3824 and LTB = 16 otherwise. Note that bi = ai for i є [0, A-1]. (ID: 11431812)

#### 4.3.7.1.4 PDSCH coding schemes

**4.3.7.1.4.0-1**  (ID: `11431831`)

PDSCH coding schemes
The PDSCH is encoded using Low Density Parity Check Coding (LDPC) scheme. The whole LDPC encoding procedure is presented mathematically in § 5.3.2 of 3GPP TS 38.212.

Two Base Graphs (BGs) have been introduced in 3GPP TS 38.212 and the maximum code block size, Kmax depends on the BG as depicted in following table.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Base Graph (BG#) | Kmax |
| 1 | 8448 |
| 2 | 3840 |

Both base graphs are supported (5G\_UP\_IF\_917) for initial transmission of a TB with coding rate R indicated by the MCS index according to 3GPP TS 38.214 § 5.1.3.1 and subsequent re-transmission of the same TB. Each code block of the TB is encoded with either LDPC BG#1 or BG#2 according to the following:

* if A<=292, or if (A<=3824 and R<=0.67), or if R<=0.25 then LDPC BG# 2 is used;
* otherwise, LDPC BG# 1 is used.

PDSCH code block segmentation and code block CRC attachment
The input bit sequence to the LDPC code block segmentation is denoted by b0, b1, …, bB-1 where B is as defined in 5G\_L1\_2051\_replaced([11431812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_GUkPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If B is larger than the maximum code block size Kmax, segmentation of the input bit sequence is performed and an additional CRC sequence of LCB=24 bits is attached to each code block.
The number of code blocks C is determined as follows:
 If B is smaller than or equal to Kmax, then there is a single code block (C=1) and in this case LCB=0 bit and the value of B’ which is the total Transport Block size after code block segmentation is equal to B (i.e. B’=B).
 Otherwise, LCB=24 bits, the Number of code blocks: [image-0][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4094.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] and B’=B+C.LCB.
 
The total number of filler bits is calculated as: Ft=C.K-B’ with K is the number of bits per code block (including the filler bits). If the number of filler bits Ft is not 0, the filler bits are added at the end of every code block e.g. each code block has a number of filler bits equals to F=Ft/C=K-K’, where K’=B’/C. The number of bits per code block K is identical for all code blocks and given by: K=22.Zc for BG#1 and K=10.Zc for BG#2 where Zc is the minimum Z value in all sets of lifting sizes in following table that [image-1][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible objects, text, or features.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4094.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | liftSizeColumn\_index |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| liftSizeSet\_index | 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 | 7 |
| 0 | 2 | 4 | 8 | 16 | 32 | 64 | 128 | 256 |
| 1 | 3 | 6 | 12 | 24 | 48 | 96 | 192 | 384 |
| 2 | 5 | 10 | 20 | 40 | 80 | 160 | 320 |  |
| 3 | 7 | 14 | 28 | 56 | 112 | 224 |  |  |
| 4 | 9 | 18 | 36 | 72 | 144 | 288 |  |  |
| 5 | 11 | 22 | 44 | 88 | 176 | 352 |  |  |
| 6 | 13 | 26 | 52 | 104 | 208 |  |  |  |
| 7 | 15 | 30 | 60 | 120 | 240 |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| The parameter Kb for BG#1 and BG#2 is given in following table: |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | BG#1 | BG#2 |
| B>640 | 22 | 10 |
| 560 |  |  |

##### 4.3.7.1.5.1 Parameters table

##### 4.3.7.1.5.1-1 Parameters table

#### 4.3.7.1.6 PDSCH LDPC encoding

**4.3.7.1.6.0-1**  (ID: `11431888`)

PDSCH LDPC encoding
The bit sequence input for a given code block to the LDPC coding is denoted by c0, c1, …, c(K-1), where K is the number of bits to encode which is identical for all code blocks.

For each code block, the K bits are encoded based on (D-K) by D parity check matrix (refer to 3GPP TS 38.212 § 5.3.2 for the mathematical presentation), where D is the number of encoded bits per output stream and D-K is the number of parity check bits.

After encoding, the bits are denoted by d0, d1, …, d(D-1). The codeword length D is equal to D=66.Zc for BG#1 and D=50.Zc for BG#2 where Zc is the value as specified during the code block segmentation in 5G\_L1\_2040\_replaced([11431839](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_GUlPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

s

---

#### 4.3.7.1.7 PDSCH rate matching

**4.3.7.1.7.0-3**  (ID: `12717828`)

PDSCH rate matching
The rate matching for LDPC code is defined per coded block and consists of bit selection and bit interleaving.

The input bit sequence to rate matching is d0, d1, …, d(D-1).

The output bit sequence after rate matching is denoted as e0, e1, …, e(E-1).

In what follows a brief overview of the rate matching mechanism is provided and one can refer to 3GPP TS 38.212 § 5.4.2 for more details.

The number of bits G available for transmission of the TB over the air interface in the considered NR-slot is in general different from C x D (C times D). The encoded code blocks therefore have to be “rate-matched” to the available air interface capacity.

Since G is an essential parameter in the rate matching process, let us first indicate how it is computed. Let us define:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| NPDSCH | The total number of PDSCH OFDM symbols available for PDSCH and its associated DMRS transmission in the NR-slot (corresponds to the parameter “numOfPdschSymbols” sent in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message. |
| NDMRS | The number of DL data OFDM symbols (Ddm) reserved for DMRS associated to PDSCH transmission in the NR-slot. |
| NDd | The number of DL data OFDM symbols (Dd) available for PDSCH transmission in the NR-slot. |
| NPRB | The number of DL PRBs allocated for transmission of the TB in the NR-slot. NPRB equals to the value of the parameter “numOfPrb” sent in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message. |
| NscRB | The number of sub-carriers per PRB (equals to 12). |
| NDLPTRS | The number of REs that cannot be used for PDSCH transmission in the NR-slot because they are used for DL PTRS transmission, within the PRBs allocated to the transmission of the TB. |
| Nlte | The number of REs not available due to LTE channels and signals mapping per layer in the NR-slot for the transmission of the TB in FDD. The default value is 0, and will be assigned to the value of parameter “numLteCrsMappingRes” sent in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message as described in ([11436231](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_PeePvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). |
| NCSI-RS | The number of REs not available due to NR CSI-RS transmission (CSI-RS for Tracking in TDD and FDD or CSI-IM pattern 0 mapped to ZP CSI-RS **for FDD DSS).** The default value is 0, and will be assigned to the value of the parameter “numCsirsMappingRes” sent in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message in case of parameter “isCsirsRateMatching” set to 1 “True”. In case of conflict between PTRS and CSI-RS for Tracking, these conflicting REs are used to transmit CSI-RS (Ct) and are not counted in the value of “numCsirsMappingRes” in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message. Instead, they shall be counted in NDLPTRS. The details refer to ([11437443](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_TI3PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). |
| NPDCCH | The number of REs not available due to NR PDCCH transmission. The default value is 0. The details refer to ([11438635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_VlGvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)). |
| G’ | The number of REs available per layer in the NR-slot for the transmission of the TB. |
| NL | The number of layers used for the transmission of the TB in the NR-slot (corresponds to the parameter “numOfLayers” sent in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message. |
| Qm | The order of the modulation used for the transmission of the TB in the NR-slot: 2 for QPSK, 4 for 16QAM, 6 for 64QAM, 8 for 256QAM and 10 for 1024QAM (corresponds to the parameter “modulationOrder” sent in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message). |

Then:

* NDd

For DMRS configuration type 1:

If numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData *= 2, N*Dd = (NPDSCH – NDMRS);

If numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData *= 1, NDd = (NPDSCH – NDMRS/2).*

For DMRS configuration type 2:

If numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData *= 3, N*Dd = (NPDSCH – NDMRS);

If numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData *= 2, N*Dd = (NPDSCH – 2\*NDMRS/3);

If numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData *= 1, NDd = (NPDSCH – NDMRS/3).*

* NDLPTRS

If PTRS are deactivated, NDLPTRS = 0

If PTRS are activated, dlPtrsFrequencyDensity = 2 or 4 (i.e. KPTRS = 2 or 4):

- If dlPtrsTimeDensity is set to 1 (i.e. LPTRS = 1), NDLPTRS = NDd.Ceiling(NPRB/dlPtrsFrequencyDensity)

- If dlPtrsTimeDensity is set to 4 (i.e. LPTRS = 4), depends on the slot format:

NDLPTRS = Floor(NDd/4).Ceiling(NPRB/dlPtrsFrequencyDensity) or

NDLPTRS = Ceiling(NDd/4).Ceiling.NPRB/dlPtrsFrequencyDensity)

For example, NDLPTRS = Floor(NDd/4).Ceiling(NPRB/4) for NR slot type B4/Id\_56/Id\_57.

* G’ = NDd  . NPRB . NscRB – NDLPTRS - Nlte- NCSI-RSe- NPDCCH
* G = G‘ . NL . Qm

The supported technology types in L1 DL **are shown in table below:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Technology** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor BB** | Odin BB |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Supported | [CB012115-SR-JA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-JA] |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-LA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-LA] |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: **Supported** | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Supported | [CB012115-SR-IA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI** | - | - | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-GA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-GA] |
| **FR2-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | - | - |  |
| **FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported |  |

---

Bit selection and pruning 11432035: Bit selection and pruning The bit sequence after encoding d0, d1, …, d(D-1) is written into a circular buffer of length Ncb bits for the r-th coded block, where D is defined in 5G_L1_2041_replaced( 11431880 ) and Ncb is provided by 5G-L2-PS to L1 with the parameter “ncb” in the DlData_PdschSendReq message. Denoting by E the rate matching output sequence length for the r-th coded block and the rate matching output bit sequence is [image-0][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_4101.001.png[/image_path][/image-0], k=0, 1, …, E-1. The code blocks after LDPC encoding all have the same number of bits D, it is natural to try and distribute the available number G’ of REs per layer equally between the C code blocks, in other words, and roughly speaking allocate (G’/C) REs to each code block, which, given the number of layers and modulation used, corresponds to (G’ NL Qm)/C = G/C bits available per code block. The purpose of rate matching is therefore to adapt the size of each code block from D bits to E ≈ G/C bits, using repetition and suppression. However, as indicated previously the actual distribution of REs between code blocks is more complex than just described above because G’/C is not necessarily an integer. When G’/C is not an integer, the number of available REs per layer can only be “nearly equally” distributed between the code blocks. If γ’ denotes the remainder in the division of G’ by C ([image-1][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_4101.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]), the (C-γ’) code blocks with index [image-2][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible objects, text, or features.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_4101.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] are rate-matched to a size of [image-3][image_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or table present.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_4101.004.png[/image_path][/image-3] and the remaining ones to a size of [image-4][image_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or table present.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_4101.005.png[/image_path][/image-4]. The value of k0 is provided by 5G-L2-PS to L1 through the parameter “k0divZ” in the DlData_PdschSendReq message, where k0divZ represents the value of k0/Zc for rvIndex=1, 2 and 3 (for rvIndex=0, k0/Zc =0). k=0; j=0; while {k &lt; E } if [image-6][image_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. #### 4.3.7.1.8 PDSCH code block concatenation

##### 4.3.7.1.8.0-1 PDSCH code block concatenation After rate matching, the rate-matched code blocks are concatenated in order to build a unique codeword for this transport block. The bits in this codeword are denoted by f0, f1, …, fG-1. (ID: 11432090)

PDSCH scrambling
The codeword is scrambled by bitwise modulo 2 addition with a scrambling sequence [image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4107.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]. The second m-sequence in the scrambling sequence generator for PDSCH should be initialized according to the following:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, light-colored font on a white background. The equation is:
$C\_{init} = n\_{RNTI} \cdot 2^{15} + q \cdot 2^{14} + n\_{ID}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4107.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image\_desc]n\_{ID} = N\_{ID}^{cell}[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4107.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

At the start of each NR-slot where:

 nRNTI is the RNTI associated with the PDSCH transmission, provided to L1 by the parameter “rnti” in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message.

 q is the index identifying the codeword. There can be only one codeword per NR-slot and hence q is always equal to zero.

 nID is equals to NIDcell. It is an integer in the range [0, 1007]. It is provided to L1 by the parameter “pdschScramblingSeqInit” in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message. 5G-L1-DL shall use “pdschScramblingSeqInit” to initialize the scrambling sequence for PDSCH for both FDD CPRI, TDD FR1 and TDD FR2 with CPRI or eCPRI fronthaul cases.

The scrambled bits in the codeword are here denoted by f0sc, f1sc, …, fG-1sc.

The supported technology types in L1 DL **are shown in table below:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Technology** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor BB** | **Odin BB** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Supported | [CB012115-SR-JA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-JA] |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-LA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-LA] |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | - | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: Supported | - |
| **FR1-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R2 onwards: **Supported** | - |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Supported | [CB012115-SR-IA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-IA] |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI** | - | - | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported | [CB012115-SR-GA] Starting from 27R3 onwards: Supported [End CB012115-SR-GA] |
| **FR2-TDD-CPRI** | Supported | - | - | - |
| **FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | Supported | Supported | Starting from 24R3 onwards: Supported | - |

---

### 4.3.7.2 PDSCH modulation

**4.3.7.2.0-1**  (ID: `13379821`)

PDSCH modulation
The scrambled bits are then modulated as described in 3GPP TS 38.214 § 5.1 using one of the specified modulation schemes resulting in a block of complex-valued modulation symbols which are denoted by {m0, m1, …}.

As indicated previously 3GPP TS 38.214 specifies tables:

* The table for MCS up to 64QAM provided in Table 5.1.3.1-1 in 3GPP TS 38.214
* The table for MCS up to 256QAM provided in Table 5.1.3.1-2 in 3GPP TS 38.214
* The table for MCS of 64QAM Low Spectral Efficiency provided in Table 5.1.3.1-3 in 3GPP TS 38.214.
* The table for MCS up to 1024QAM provided in Table 5.1.3.1-4 in 3GPP TS 38.214

The selection of the table depends on the setting of a RRC parameter MCS-Table-PDSCH which itself depends on the gNB and the UE capabilities. The supported MCS tables in gNB see [11431652: PDSCH MCS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_FtefvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

All code block segmentation parameters as indicated in ([11431871](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_G7kfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

PDSCH layer mapping
The complex-valued modulation symbols are mapped onto one or several layers according to Table 7.3.1.3-1 in 3GPP TS 38.211 [1].

Only one codeword is considered and will be mapped to 1 to 4 layers.

The complex-valued modulation symbols mapped on layer k are denoted by x(k)(i), with k є [0, ν-1] and i є [0, [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains text that is blurry and difficult to read. The visible text appears to be "M layer" and "symb".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4111.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]-1], and where v denotes the number of layers (1..4) and corresponds to the parameter “numOfLayers” in the DlData\_PdschSendReq message. [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains text that is blurry and difficult to read. The visible text appears to be "M layer" and "symb".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4111.001.png[/image\_path][/image-1] is the number of molulation symbols per layer.

 When the number of layers is one, the layer mapping operation does nothing. The sequence of modulation symbols obtained after modulation is simply sent on the single available layer.

 When the number of layers is two, modulation symbols with even index in the sequence are sent on the first layer (layer 0) and modulation symbols with odd index in the sequence are sent on the second layer (layer 1):

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4111.002.png[/image\_path][/image-2] and [image-3][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible objects, text, or features.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4111.003.png[/image\_path][/image-3].

 When the number of layers is three, the modulation symbols sequence are:
