For numLteCrsPorts = 1
5G-L1-DL shall support 1 port lteCrs in subcell type A1.
 
The REs (OFDM symbol [image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4319.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0], subcarrier [image-1][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4319.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]) overlapping between NR PDSCH and LTE-CRS in shared normal LTE subframes are listed by the following equation.
 
For deltaRbNrLte <= m < dlBandwidth
[image-2][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4319.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] = 6*m + (nu + reLteNuShift) mod 6
For dlBandwidth <= m < (2*dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)
[image-3][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4319.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] = 1 + 6*m + (nu + reLteNuShift) mod 6
 
where:
deltaRbNrLte = (dlBandwidth – lteDlBandwidth)
if numLteCrsPorts = 1,
       for l = 4 and 11, nu = 3
       for l = 7, nu = 0
[image-4][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4319.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] belongs to OFDM symbol range: [0..13]
[image-5][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4319.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5] belongs to subcarrier range: [0..(12*dlBandwidth – 1)] starting from CRB 0 (point A)
 
Note: The central frequency is bypassed in DL baseband OFDM signal in LTE (see 36.211 §6.12).
 
[xlsx\_desc]--- Sheet1 ---
NaN NaN NaN deltaRbNrLte<=m

---

###### 4.3.7.6.1.2.1.1 PDSCH REs mapping for LTE-CRS rate matching

###### 4.3.7.6.1.2.1.1.1 Configuration

**4.3.7.6.1.2.1.1.1.0-2**  (ID: `11436461`)

Configuration
If the lteCrsOption IE is set to 1 (LTECRSMAPPING) in DlData\_PdschSendReq, the number of LTE-CRS REs is computed by 5G-L2-PS and is provided to 5G-L1-DL through numLteCrsMappingRes IE in DlData\_PdschSendReq for each PDSCH granted. 5G-L1-DL shall withdraw the LTE-CRS mapped REs (see 5G\_L1\_2164\_replaced([11436290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_PefvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L1\_2165\_replaced([11436362](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_QFgfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for NR PDSCH mapping to avoid collision. Used for the scenario of transferring PDSCH in shared normal LTE subframes.

NR PDSCH shall be configured as below:

* In case of ld=14:

 PDSCH mapping type A, PDSCH duration ld=14

o First 2 symbols for LTE PDCCH, 1 symbol for NR PDCCH (3rd symbol in slot), 11 symbols for NR PDSCH.

 DMRS configuration:

o dlDmrsTypeAPos = 3

o dlDmrsAddPos = 0 (only FL DMRS) or 1 (1 additional DMRS). The additional DMRS position is 12.

o numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData = 1 or 2

**[PR719206]**

* In case of ld=12:

 PDSCH mapping type A, PDSCH duration ld=12

o First 2 symbols for LTE for PDCCH, 1 symbol for NR PDCCH (3rd symbol in slot), 9 symbols for NR PDSCH, last 2 symbols unused.

 DMRS configuration:

o dlDmrsTypeAPos(l0) = 3

o dlDmrsAddPos = 2 (2 additional DMRS). The additional DMRS are located on the 6th and 9th OFDM symbols in slot.

**[End PR719206]**

Note: for FDD DSS, PTRS is disabled for PDSCH with LTE CRS rate matching.

The supported technology types in L1 DL **are shown in table below:**

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | - | Supported | - |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | - | Supported | - |

---

Case: numLteCrsPorts = 1
For FDD, if the carrier bandwidth is an even number, PDSCH RE mapping for rate matching around LTE-CRS in shared LTE normal subframes with numLteCrsPorts=1.

Case 1:  numLteCrsPorts=1, PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS without additional DMRS or with one additional DMRS.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a visual representation of resource element (RE) allocation for LTE and NR (New Radio) systems, specifically focusing on the distribution of data and Demodulation Reference Signals (DMRS) within Resource Blocks (RBs). The diagram illustrates how these REs are mapped across subcarriers and symbols for different configurations of `reLteNuShift`.
The legend on the top left explains the color coding:
- Red squares: LTE-CRS RE (Cell-specific Reference Signal)
- White squares: RE not used for NR PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel)
- Green squares: NR PDSCH data RE
- Yellow squares: NR FL DMRS RE (Front-loaded Demodulation Reference Signal)
- Purple squares: NR additional DMRS RE if `IDmrsAddPos` = 1
- Blue squares: NR PDSCH data RE if `dIDmrsAddPos` = 0
The main part of the image consists of six columns, each representing a different value of `reLteNuShift` from 0 to 5. Each column shows a grid of Resource Blocks (RBs). The vertical axis represents the ARFCN frequency, with RBs above and below the central subcarrier. The horizontal axis represents the subcarriers within an RB, numbered from 0 to 13. Each RB is further divided into two slots, each containing 7 symbols.
The central RB is highlighted with a red bracket and labeled "Central RB". The central subcarrier is also indicated.
Each of the six columns depicts the RE allocation for a specific `reLteNuShift` value:
- **reLteNuShift = 0**: Shows a pattern of red squares (LTE-CRS) in the first and last subcarriers of the first and last RBs. Green squares (NR PDSCH data) fill most of the remaining REs. Yellow squares (NR FL DMRS) are present in the second subcarrier. Purple squares (NR additional DMRS) are in the third subcarrier.
- **reLteNuShift = 1**: Similar to `reLteNuShift = 0`, but the positions of some red squares (LTE-CRS) and purple squares (NR additional DMRS) are shifted.
- **reLteNuShift = 2**: Shows a different distribution of red squares (LTE-CRS) and purple squares (NR additional DMRS) compared to the previous configurations.
- **reLteNuShift = 3**: Displays another variation in the placement of red and purple squares.
- **reLteNuShift = 4**: Further variations in the red and purple square placements are observed.
- **reLteNuShift = 5**: The final configuration shows a distinct pattern of red and purple squares.
Above the grids, there is a formula: `k = 6*(2*dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)`. This formula likely relates to the calculation of a parameter `k` based on downlink bandwidth and another parameter `deltaRbNrLte`, which might influence the DMRS positioning or other resource allocation aspects.
The image also shows smaller, zoomed-in views of the top and bottom parts of the RB grids, likely to provide a clearer view of the RE distribution in those specific areas.
The labels below each column indicate "NR-slot=14 symbols" and the corresponding `reLteNuShift` value.
The overall image aims to illustrate the flexibility and configuration options in NR PDSCH RE allocation, particularly concerning the placement of DMRS and the interaction with LTE CRS.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4334.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[PR719206]

Case 2:  numLteCrsPorts=1, PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS with two additional DMRS.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the resource allocation for LTE-CRS and NR PDSCH within a central resource block (RB) and surrounding RBs. The diagram is organized into several columns, each representing a different value of `reLteNuShift` from 0 to 5. Each column contains two sub-diagrams, one above and one below the "central RB" line, indicating RBs above and below the central frequency.
Each sub-diagram is a grid representing resource elements (REs) within a slot. The rows of the grid are labeled from 0 to 11, representing subcarriers, and the columns are labeled from 0 to 9, representing symbols within a slot. Different colors denote different types of REs:
- Red squares represent LTE-CRS REs.
- White squares represent REs not used for NR PDSCH.
- Green squares represent NR PDSCH data REs.
- Yellow squares represent NR FL DMRS REs.
- Purple squares represent NR additional DMRS REs.
A legend at the top left of the image clarifies these color codes.
The central RB is highlighted with a red bracket and the label "central RB". The vertical axis is labeled "RBs: above the ARFCN frequency" for the upper section and "RBs: below the ARFCN frequency" for the lower section.
The columns are further distinguished by the label "NR-slot=14 symbols" and the corresponding `reLteNuShift` value.
At the top of the image, there is a formula: `k = 6 * (2 * dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)`. This formula is connected by arrows to two smaller grids, which appear to be simplified representations of resource allocation.
The overall diagram illustrates how the placement of NR PDSCH data REs, NR FL DMRS REs, and NR additional DMRS REs varies with the `reLteNuShift` parameter, while LTE-CRS REs remain fixed.
The text in the image is:

NR additianal DMRS RE
k = 6*(2*dlBandwidth deltaRbNrLte)

[End PR719206]

The supported technology types in L1 DL are shown in table below:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Loner | Loki | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | - | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | - | - | - |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | - | Supported | - |

Case: numLteCrsPorts = 1
For FDD, if the carrier bandwidth is an odd number, PDSCH RE mapping for LTE-CRS rate matching in shared LTE normal subframes with numLteCrsPorts=1.

Case 1:  numLteCrsPorts=1, PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS without additional DMRS or with one additional DMRS.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a visual representation of resource element (RE) allocation in a cellular communication system, likely 5G NR (New Radio) and LTE (Long-Term Evolution). The diagram illustrates how different types of resource elements are distributed across Resource Blocks (RBs) and symbols within a slot.
The legend on the top left explains the color coding of the resource elements:
- **White:** LTE-CRS RE (Cell-specific Reference Signal)
- **Yellow:** RE not used for NR PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel)
- **Green:** NR PDSCH data RE
- **Orange:** NR FL DMRS RE (Demodulation Reference Signal)
- **Purple:** NR additional DMRS RE if IDmrsAddPos = 1
- **Red:** NR PDSCH data RE if dIDmrsAddPos = 0
The main part of the image consists of several columns, each representing a different value of `reLteNuShift` from 0 to 5. Each column shows a grid of resource elements. The vertical axis represents RBs, with "RBs: below the ARFCN frequency" at the bottom and "RBs: above the ARFCN frequency" at the top. The horizontal axis represents symbols within a slot, labeled from 0 to 13. The central RB is indicated by a red arrow and the label "central subcarrier".
Each column depicts a specific configuration of resource elements. The distribution of green (NR PDSCH data RE) and orange (NR FL DMRS RE) squares varies across the columns, indicating different allocation patterns based on the `reLteNuShift` parameter. The purple and red squares represent specific conditions for additional DMRS and PDSCH data REs, respectively.
At the top right, there is a formula: `k = 6*(2*dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)`. This formula likely relates to the calculation of some parameter within the system, possibly related to bandwidth or resource allocation.
The bottom of each column indicates "NR-slot=14 symbols" and the corresponding `reLteNuShift` value.
The image is a detailed technical diagram illustrating resource mapping in a wireless communication system.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4337.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[PR719206]

Case 2:  numLteCrsPorts=1, PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS with two additional DMRS.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a visual representation of resource element (RE) allocation in a communication system, likely related to LTE and NR (New Radio) standards. The diagram illustrates how different types of resource elements are distributed across subcarriers and symbols within resource blocks (RBs).
The key elements are:
\* **Legend:** A legend on the top left defines the color coding for different RE types:
\* Red: LTE-CRS RE (Cell-specific Reference Signal)
\* White: RE not used for NR PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel)
\* Green: NR PDSCH data RE
\* Yellow: NR FL DMRS RE (Demodulation Reference Signal)
\* Purple: NR additional DMRS RE
\* **Frequency Axis:** The vertical axis is labeled "RBs: above the ARFCN frequency" and "RBs: below the ARFCN frequency," indicating frequency allocation. A "Central RB" and "central subcarrier" are highlighted.
\* **Time Axis:** The horizontal axis within each grid represents subcarriers, numbered from 0 to 13. The vertical axis within each grid represents symbols, numbered from 0 to 11.
\* **Resource Block Grids:** The main part of the image consists of several grids, each representing a resource block. These grids show the distribution of the RE types described in the legend.
\* Each grid is divided into two halves vertically, representing two slots within a symbol.
\* The distribution of red (LTE-CRS), green (NR PDSCH data), yellow (NR FL DMRS), and purple (NR additional DMRS) REs varies across the grids.
\* There are six main grids shown side-by-side, each labeled with "NR-slot=14 symbols" and a "reLteNuShift" value from 0 to 5. This suggests different configurations or shifts in the resource allocation.
\* There are also smaller grids at the top, likely representing a different configuration or a part of a larger structure.
\* **Formula:** A formula is displayed at the top right: `k = 6 * (2 * dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)`. This formula likely relates to the calculation of a parameter `k` based on downlink bandwidth and a delta value for NR-LTE.
The image demonstrates how different reference signals and data channels are placed within the time-frequency grid, with variations based on the `reLteNuShift` parameter. The presence of both LTE-CRS and NR signals suggests a scenario where both technologies might coexist or be managed within the same spectrum.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4337.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[End PR719206]

The supported technology types in L1 DL are shown in table below:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Loner | Loki | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | - | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | - | - | - |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | - | Supported | - |

For FDD, if the carrier bandwidth is an even number, PDSCH RE mapping with LTE-CRS rate matching in shared LTE normal subframes with numLteCrsPorts=2.

Case 1:  numLteCrsPorts=2, PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS without additional DMRS or with one additional DMRS.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating Resource Element (RE) allocation for LTE and NR (New Radio) systems, specifically focusing on PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) and DMRS (Demodulation Reference Signal).
The diagram is divided into several sections:
1. **Legend:** A legend at the top left explains the color coding for different types of Resource Elements:
\* Red: LTE-CRS RE (Cell Reference Signal)
\* White: RE not used for NR PDSCH
\* Green: NR PDSCH data RE
\* Yellow: NR FL DMRS RE (Front-Load DMRS)
\* Purple: NR additional DMRS RE if IDmrsAddPos = 1
\* Green (with a note): NR PDSCH data RE if dIDmrsAddPos = 0
2. **Formula:** A formula is presented at the top: `k = 6*(2*dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)`.
3. **Resource Grid Diagrams:** Three main resource grid diagrams are shown, each representing a different configuration of `reLteNuShift`. Each grid is composed of Resource Blocks (RBs) arranged vertically and symbols arranged horizontally. The vertical axis is labeled "RBs: above the ARFCN frequency" and "RBs: below the ARFCN frequency", with a "Central RB" and "central subcarrier" indicated. The horizontal axis is labeled "NR-slot=14 symbols" and ranges from 0 to 13.
\* **Left Diagram:** Labeled "reLteNuShift = 0 or 3". This grid shows a distribution of LTE-CRS (red squares), NR PDSCH data (green squares), NR FL DMRS (yellow column), and NR additional DMRS (purple column).
\* **Middle Diagram:** Labeled "reLteNuShift = 1 or 4". This grid shows a similar distribution of REs, with slight variations in the placement of NR PDSCH data REs compared to the left diagram.
\* **Right Diagram:** Labeled "reLteNuShift = 2 or 5". This grid also displays the RE allocation, with further variations in the NR PDSCH data RE placement.
4. **Upper Resource Grid:** Above the three main diagrams, there is a smaller resource grid diagram with a "......" indicating repetition. This likely represents a different view or a continuation of the resource allocation.
The diagrams visually represent how different types of resource elements are mapped within a time-frequency grid for NR PDSCH transmission, considering different shift configurations. The color coding and the presence of specific REs (like DMRS) are crucial for signal demodulation and data reception.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4341.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[PR719206]

Case 2:  numLteCrsPorts=2, PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS with two additional DMRS.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating Resource Element (RE) allocation for LTE-CRS and NR PDSCH, along with NR DMRS. The diagram is divided into three main sections, each representing a different configuration of `reLteNuShift`. Each section contains a grid that visualizes Resource Blocks (RBs) across the ARFCN frequency and subcarriers.
The legend at the top left defines the color coding for different RE types:
- Red: LTE-CRS RE
- White: RE not used for NR PDSCH
- Green: NR PDSCH data RE
- Yellow: NR FL DMRS RE
- Purple: NR additional DMRS RE
The diagram also includes a formula at the top: `k = 6 * (2 * dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)`.
Each of the three main sections shows a grid of Resource Blocks. The vertical axis represents RBs, with "RBs: above the ARFCN frequency" and "RBs: below the ARFCN frequency" indicated. The horizontal axis represents subcarriers within a Resource Block, numbered from 0 to 13. The central RB and central subcarrier are highlighted.
The three configurations are:
1. **`reLteNuShift = 0 or 3`**: This section shows two grids, one above the other, representing NR-slot=14 symbols. The top grid has a specific pattern of red (LTE-CRS), green (NR PDSCH data), yellow (NR FL DMRS), and purple (NR additional DMRS) REs. The bottom grid shows a similar pattern.
2. **`reLteNuShift = 1 or 4`**: This section also displays two grids for NR-slot=14 symbols. The RE allocation pattern differs from the first configuration.
3. **`reLteNuShift = 2 or 5`**: This section, similar to the others, shows two grids for NR-slot=14 symbols with a distinct RE allocation pattern.
A smaller grid at the top right, with an arrow pointing to it from the formula, seems to illustrate a specific part of the calculation or a representative RB allocation.
The overall diagram aims to explain how different REs are mapped within Resource Blocks for LTE and NR coexistence scenarios, with variations based on the `reLteNuShift` parameter.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4341.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[End PR719206]

Case: numLteCrsPorts = 2
For FDD, if the carrier bandwidth is an odd number, PDSCH RE mapping for LTE-CRS rate matching in shared LTE normal subframes with numLteCrsPorts=2.

Case 1:  numLteCrsPorts=2, PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS without additional DMRS or with one additional DMRS.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating Resource Element (RE) allocation for LTE-CRS and NR PDSCH, along with NR DMRS. The diagram is divided into three main sections, each representing a different configuration of `reLteNuShift`. A legend at the top explains the color coding for different types of REs: red for LTE-CRS RE, white for RE not used for NR PDSCH, green for NR PDSCH data RE, orange for NR FL DMRS RE, and purple for NR additional DMRS RE (if `IDmrsAddPos` = 1) or NR PDSCH data RE (if `dIDmrsAddPos` = 0).
Each of the three main sections is a grid representing Resource Blocks (RBs) across the ARFCN frequency. The vertical axis is labeled "RBs: above the ARFCN frequency" and "RBs: below the ARFCN frequency", with numbers from 0 to 11 indicating the RB index. The horizontal axis is labeled with numbers from 0 to 13, representing symbols within a slot. The central RB is highlighted with a red box and an arrow pointing to it, with "central subcarrier" labeled next to it.
The top right corner shows a smaller, zoomed-in view of a portion of the grid, illustrating the placement of REs.
Below each of the three main grid sections, there is text indicating "NR-slot=14 symbols" and the corresponding `reLteNuShift` value:
- The leftmost section has `reLteNuShift = 0 or 3`.
- The middle section has `reLteNuShift = 1 or 4`.
- The rightmost section has `reLteNuShift = 2 or 5`.
The formula `k = 6*(2*dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)` is displayed at the top of the image, with a blue arrow pointing from it towards the zoomed-in grid section.
The grids themselves show a pattern of colored squares representing the REs. The orange column appears to be consistently on the left side of the data region, followed by green squares (NR PDSCH data RE) and purple squares on the right. Red squares (LTE-CRS RE) are scattered within the green areas. The white squares indicate unused REs. The distribution of these colored squares varies slightly across the three main configurations, reflecting the different `reLteNuShift` values.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4344.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[PR719206]

Case 2:  numLteCrsPorts=2, PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS with two additional DMRS.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating Resource Element (RE) allocation for LTE and NR (New Radio) systems, specifically focusing on PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) and DMRS (Demodulation Reference Signal) configurations.
At the top, a formula is presented:
`k = 6 * (2 * dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)`
Below the formula is a legend explaining the color coding of the Resource Elements:
\* **Red:** LTE-CRS RE (Cell-specific Reference Signal)
\* **Yellow:** RE not used for NR PDSCH
\* **Green:** NR PDSCH data RE
\* **Orange:** NR FL DMRS RE (First-order Demodulation Reference Signal)
\* **Purple:** NR additional DMRS RE
The main part of the image consists of three grid-like diagrams, each representing a set of Resource Blocks (RBs) across 14 symbols (time domain) and 13 RBs (frequency domain). The RBs are labeled from 0 to 13 on the x-axis, and symbols are labeled from 0 to 11 on the y-axis. The diagrams are arranged horizontally.
The y-axis on the left is labeled "RBs: above the ARFCN frequency" and "RBs: below the ARFCN frequency", indicating the frequency domain. A vertical arrow points to the "Central RB" and "central subcarrier", highlighting a specific RB.
Each of the three main diagrams shows a different configuration of REs. The diagrams are further categorized by the "NR-slot=14 symbols" and "reLteNuShift" values displayed below them:
1. **Left Diagram:** `reLteNuShift = 0 or 3`
This diagram shows a central RB with a specific allocation. The LTE-CRS REs (red) are present in the first column. The NR PDSCH data REs (green) occupy a significant portion of the RBs. NR FL DMRS REs (orange) are in the second column, and NR additional DMRS REs (purple) are in the third and fourth columns. Red squares within the green and purple areas represent specific REs.
2. **Middle Diagram:** `reLteNuShift = 1 or 4`
This diagram also shows a central RB. The pattern of LTE-CRS REs, NR PDSCH data REs, NR FL DMRS REs, and NR additional DMRS REs is similar to the left diagram, but the placement of the red squares (specific REs) differs.
3. **Right Diagram:** `reLteNuShift = 2 or 5`
This diagram presents another configuration. The general distribution of colored REs is consistent, but the red squares are placed differently compared to the other two diagrams.
A smaller diagram is shown in the top right corner, with a blue arrow pointing to it from the main diagrams. This smaller diagram appears to be a zoomed-in or representative view of a portion of the RB allocation, showing a similar pattern of colored REs and red squares. Below this smaller diagram, there are three dots (`...`), suggesting that there are more such configurations or that this is a continuation of the pattern.
The overall image aims to visually represent how different types of reference signals and data channels are mapped onto the time-frequency grid in a mixed LTE and NR environment, with variations based on the `reLteNuShift` parameter.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4344.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[End PR719206]

The supported technology types in L1 DL are shown in table below:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Loner | Loki | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | - | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | - | Supported | - |

---

For FDD, if the carrier bandwidth is an even number, PDSCH RE mapping for LTE-CRS rate matching in shared LTE normal subframes with numLteCrsPorts=4.

Case 1:  numLteCrsPorts=4, PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS without additional DMRS or with one additional DMRS.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating Resource Element (RE) allocation for NR PDSCH (New Radio Physical Downlink Shared Channel) in relation to LTE-CRS (Long Term Evolution - Cell Reference Signal). The diagram is divided into three main sections, each representing a different configuration of `reLteNuShift` values (0 or 3, 1 or 4, and 2 or 5). Each section contains a grid representing Resource Blocks (RBs) and subcarriers, with different colors indicating the type of RE.
A legend on the top left explains the color coding:
\* **Red:** LTE-CRS RE
\* **White:** RE not used for NR PDSCH
\* **Green:** NR PDSCH data RE
\* **Yellow:** NR FL DMRS RE (Front-loaded Demodulation Reference Signal)
\* **Purple:** NR additional DMRS RE if `dIdmrsAddPos` = 1
\* **Green (with a note):** NR PDSCH data RE if `dIdmrsAddPos` = 0
The vertical axis is labeled "RBs: above the ARFCN frequency" and "RBs: below the ARFCN frequency", with a central section highlighted as "Central RB" and "central subcarrier". The horizontal axis in each grid is labeled with numbers from 0 to 13, representing subcarriers within a Resource Block. The top right corner shows a formula: `k = 6*(2*dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)`.
Each of the three main sections depicts a 14-symbol slot for NR. The differences between the sections lie in the placement of the DMRS REs and the NR PDSCH data REs, which are influenced by the `reLteNuShift` parameter.
**Section 1 (reLteNuShift = 0 or 3):**
This section shows a grid with RBs and subcarriers. The first subcarrier (0) is primarily occupied by LTE-CRS REs (red). Subcarrier 1 contains NR FL DMRS REs (yellow). Subcarriers 2 through 11 are predominantly NR PDSCH data REs (green), with some red squares indicating LTE-CRS REs. Subcarrier 12 contains additional DMRS REs (purple). There are also some white REs indicating unused resources.
**Section 2 (reLteNuShift = 1 or 4):**
Similar to Section 1, this section also shows a 14-symbol slot. The distribution of REs is slightly different, particularly in the placement of the green NR PDSCH data REs and the red LTE-CRS REs. The yellow NR FL DMRS REs are in subcarrier 1, and purple additional DMRS REs are in subcarrier 12.
**Section 3 (reLteNuShift = 2 or 5):**
This section presents another variation in RE allocation. The yellow NR FL DMRS REs are in subcarrier 1, and purple additional DMRS REs are in subcarrier 12. The green NR PDSCH data REs and red LTE-CRS REs are distributed differently compared to the other two sections.
The diagram effectively visualizes how different parameters affect the resource allocation for NR PDSCH within a given bandwidth and slot configuration.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4348.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[PR719206]

Case 2:  numLteCrsPorts=4, PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS with two additional DMRS.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating resource element (RE) allocation for LTE and NR (New Radio) systems. The diagram is divided into three main sections, each representing a different configuration of `reLteNuShift`. Each section contains a grid of resource blocks (RBs) and symbols, with different colors indicating the type of RE.
A legend at the top left explains the color coding:
- Red: LTE-CRS RE (Cell Reference Signal)
- White: RE not used for NR PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel)
- Green: NR PDSCH data RE
- Yellow: NR FL DMRS RE (Demodulation Reference Signal)
- Purple: NR additional DMRS RE
The diagram also includes a formula at the top: `k = 6 * (2 * dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)`.
The three main sections are labeled at the bottom with `NR-slot=14 symbols` and different values for `reLteNuShift`:
1. Left section: `reLteNuShift = 0 or 3`
2. Middle section: `reLteNuShift = 1 or 4`
3. Right section: `reLteNuShift = 2 or 5`
Each section shows a grid representing resource blocks (RBs) on the y-axis (from 0 to 11) and symbols on the x-axis (from 0 to 13). The central RB and central subcarrier are indicated by a red bracket and arrow on the left side of the diagram. The RBs are described as being "above the ARFCN frequency" and "below the ARFCN frequency".
A smaller inset diagram in the top right corner shows a magnified view of a portion of the resource grid, highlighting the distribution of different RE types.
The distribution of colored REs varies across the three main sections, indicating different allocation patterns based on the `reLteNuShift` parameter. For instance, the yellow (NR FL DMRS RE) and purple (NR additional DMRS RE) blocks appear in different positions and quantities in each section. The green (NR PDSCH data RE) blocks occupy the majority of the available REs. Red blocks (LTE-CRS RE) are present in specific locations, and white blocks (RE not used for NR PDSCH) are also shown.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4348.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[End PR719206]

The supported technology types in L1 DL are shown in table below:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Loner | Loki | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | - | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | - | Supported | - |

For FDD, if the carrier bandwidth is an odd number, PDSCH RE mapping for LTE-CRS rate matching in shared LTE normal subframes with  numLteCrsPorts=4.

Case 1:  numLteCrsPorts=4, PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS without additional DMRS or with one additional DMRS.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating Resource Element (RE) allocation for LTE-CRS and NR PDSCH, along with DMRS REs. The diagram is divided into three main sections, each representing a different configuration of `reLteNuShift`. A legend on the top left explains the color coding for different types of REs:
\* **Red:** LTE-CRS RE
\* **White:** RE not used for NR PDSCH
\* **Green:** NR PDSCH data RE
\* **Yellow:** NR FL DMRS RE
\* **Purple:** NR additional DMRS RE if `IDmrsAddPos = 1`
\* **Green (with red squares):** NR PDSCH data RE if `dIDmrsAddPos = 0`
The diagram also shows a formula at the top: `k = 6*(2*dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)`.
The main body of the image consists of three grid-like representations of Resource Blocks (RBs). Each grid has 14 rows (labeled 0 to 13) representing symbols within a slot, and 14 columns (labeled 0 to 13) representing subcarriers within an RB. The vertical axis is labeled "RBs: above the ARFCN frequency" and "RBs: below the ARFCN frequency", with a "Central RB" and "central subcarrier" indicated by a red bracket and arrow.
Each of the three main sections corresponds to a specific `reLteNuShift` value:
1. **Left Section:** `reLteNuShift = 0 or 3`. This section shows a grid with yellow and purple columns on the left, followed by green and white cells. Red squares are scattered within the green cells, indicating NR PDSCH data REs.
2. **Middle Section:** `reLteNuShift = 1 or 4`. This section is similar to the left section, with yellow and purple columns on the left, followed by green and white cells. Red squares are distributed within the green cells.
3. **Right Section:** `reLteNuShift = 2 or 5`. This section also features yellow and purple columns on the left, followed by green and white cells, with red squares indicating NR PDSCH data REs.
A smaller grid is shown in the top right corner, which appears to be a zoomed-in or alternative view of a portion of the RB allocation. It also uses the same color coding.
Below each of the three main sections, it is indicated that "NR-slot=14 symbols" and the corresponding `reLteNuShift` values.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4351.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[PR719206]

Case 2:  numLteCrsPorts=4, PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS with two additional DMRS.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating resource element (RE) allocation for LTE-CRS and NR PDSCH/DMRS within resource blocks (RBs). The diagram is divided into three main columns, each representing a different configuration of `reLteNuShift` values (0 or 3, 1 or 4, and 2 or 5). Each column contains two sub-grids, one above and one below a central horizontal line labeled "Central RB" and "central subcarrier". The vertical axis of these sub-grids represents subcarriers (numbered 0 to 11), and the horizontal axis represents resource blocks (numbered 0 to 13).
A legend on the top left explains the color coding of the REs:
- Red: LTE-CRS RE
- White: RE not used for NR PDSCH
- Green: NR PDSCH data RE
- Yellow: NR FL DMRS RE
- Purple: NR additional DMRS RE
The top right corner shows a formula: `k = 6 * (2 * dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)`.
Each of the three main columns depicts the RB allocation for 14 symbols per NR slot. The sub-grids above the central line represent RBs above the ARFCN frequency, and those below represent RBs below the ARFCN frequency.
Within each sub-grid, the distribution of colored REs varies based on the `reLteNuShift` value. The yellow and purple REs (DMRS) are placed in specific patterns within the green REs (PDSCH data). Red REs (LTE-CRS) are also present, particularly at the edges of the allocated RBs. The white REs indicate unused resource elements.
The three configurations shown are:
1. `reLteNuShift = 0 or 3`: The first column shows this configuration.
2. `reLteNuShift = 1 or 4`: The second column shows this configuration.
3. `reLteNuShift = 2 or 5`: The third column shows this configuration.
The diagram highlights how different `reLteNuShift` values affect the placement of NR PDSCH data and DMRS REs relative to LTE-CRS REs and unused REs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4351.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[End PR719206]

The supported technology types in L1 DL are shown in table below:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Loner | Loki | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | - | Supported | - |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | - | Supported | - |

---

###### 4.3.7.6.1.2.1.2 PDSCH REs puncturing with LTE-CRS REs

**4.3.7.6.1.2.1.2.0-1**  (ID: `11436843`)

PDSCH REs puncturing with LTE-CRS REs
If the lteCrsOption IE is set to 2 (LTECRSPUNCTURING) in DlData\_PdschSendReq, 5G-L1-DL shall puncture the LTE-CRS mapped REs (5G\_L1\_2164\_replaced([11436290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_PefvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L1\_2165\_replaced([11436362](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_QFgfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in each RB of the PDSCH granted to avoid collision.

This solution is used for the transferring PDSCH scheduled by DCI format 1\_0 in shared normal LTE subframes with following cases:

 NSA mode: RACH Msg2/Msg4.

 SA mode: RACH Msg2/Msg4, RACH Msg4, SIB1, PAGING, and SI messages.

PDSCH can be configured as below:

 PDSCH mapping type A, PDSCH duration ld=12

o Slot type (Id\_58): (-) (-) Dc Dd Dd Dd Dd Dd Dd Dd Dd Dd (-) (-)

o First 2 symbols for LTE for PDCCH, 1 symbol for NR PDCCH (3rd symbol in slot), 9 symbols for NR PDSCH, last 2 symbols unused.

 DMRS configuration:

o dlDmrsTypeAPos(l0) = 3

o dlDmrsAddPos = 2 (2 additional DMRS). The additional DMRS are located on the 6th and 9th OFDM symbols in slot.

For FDD CPRI, the supported L1 HW:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | numLteCrsPorts = 2 (5G\_L1\_2182\_replaced([11436973](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_R6s_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | numLteCrsPorts = 4 (5G\_L1\_2184\_replaced([11437025](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_R6tvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | numLteCrsPorts = 1 (5G\_L1\_2183\_replaced([11436918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_R6sPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) |
| Loner | supported | supported | - |
| Loki | [CB006895-A] Supported in ABIO [End CB006895-A]   [CB007271-C] Supported in ABIN [End CB007271-C]   [CB009273-A] Supported in ABOE [End CB009273-A] | [CB006895-A] Supported in ABIO [End CB006895-A]   [CB007271-C] Supported in ABIN [End CB007271-C]   [CB009273-A] Supported in ABOE [End CB009273-A] | [CB008983-B] Supported in ABIO/ABIN/ASOE [End CB008983-B] |
| Thor | [RP003187-L] supported [End RP003187-L] | [RP003187-L] supported [End RP003187-L] | - |

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains the text "numLteCrsPorts = 1". The text is blurred and appears to be from a computer screen or a document.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_4363.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]. [image-1][image_desc]The image displays a visual representation of resource element (RE) allocation in a cellular communication system, likely 5G NR (New Radio) and LTE (Long-Term Evolution). The main part of the image consists of several sub-figures, each depicting a grid of resource blocks (RBs) over symbols. These sub-figures are arranged horizontally and are labeled with "NR-slot=14 symbols" and "reLteNuShift" values ranging from 0 to 5. A legend on the top left explains the color coding of the resource elements: - Red squares: NR RE punctured due to LTE-CRS RE - Yellow squares: RE not used for NR PDSCH - Green squares: NR PDSCH data RE - Orange squares: NR FL DMRS RE - Purple squares: NR additional DMRS RE The vertical axis of each grid is labeled "RBs: above the ARFCN frequency" and "RBs: below the ARFCN frequency", with a central RB indicated. The horizontal axis is labeled with numbers from 0 to 13, representing resource elements within a slot. There are also two smaller grids at the top right, above the main row of sub-figures, which appear to be illustrative examples of resource allocation. A mathematical formula is also present: "k = 6 (2 dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)". The sub-figures show variations in the placement of red (punctured) and purple (additional DMRS) resource elements as the "reLteNuShift" value changes from 0 to 5. The green (PDSCH data) and orange (FL DMRS) elements also shift their positions accordingly. The yellow (unused) elements are consistently placed at the edges of the resource grid. The overall image illustrates how the resource element allocation, particularly for DMRS and punctured REs, changes based on a parameter called "reLteNuShift", which likely relates to the synchronization or timing offset between LTE and NR systems. The formula suggests a relationship between the number of resource blocks (k), the downlink bandwidth (dlBandwidth), and a delta value (deltaRbNrLte).[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_4363.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] (ID: 11436962)

Case: numLteCrsPorts = 2
For FDD, if the carrier bandwidth is an odd number, PDSCH RE puncturing with LTE-CRS REs in shared LTE normal subframes with [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "numLteCrsPorts = 2". The text is slightly blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4366.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0].

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating resource element (RE) allocation for NR (New Radio) and LTE (Long-Term Evolution) systems, focusing on the interaction between them. The diagram is divided into three main sections, each representing a different configuration of "reLteNuShift" values.
At the top of the image, a formula is presented: "k = 6*(2*dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)". This formula likely relates to the calculation of some parameter 'k' based on bandwidth and resource block differences between NR and LTE.
Below the formula, a legend explains the color-coding used in the resource grid diagrams:
- Red squares: NR RE punctured due to LTE-CRS RE
- Light green squares: RE not used for NR PDSCH
- Dark green squares: NR PDSCH data RE
- Yellow squares: NR FL DMRS RE
- Purple squares: NR additional DMRS RE
The main part of the image consists of three resource grid diagrams arranged horizontally. Each grid represents a portion of the frequency spectrum, with the vertical axis labeled "RBs: above the ARFCN frequency" and "RBs: below the ARFCN frequency", indicating Resource Blocks relative to a central frequency. The horizontal axis is labeled with numbers from 0 to 13, representing symbols within a slot. The central subcarrier is also indicated.
Each of the three resource grid diagrams shows a pattern of colored squares, representing the allocation of different types of resource elements. The diagrams are distinguished by the "reLteNuShift" value indicated below each one:
- The leftmost diagram corresponds to "reLteNuShift = 0 or 3".
- The middle diagram corresponds to "reLteNuShift = 1 or 4".
- The rightmost diagram corresponds to "reLteNuShift = 2 or 5".
There are also two smaller resource grid diagrams shown above the main three, which appear to be zoomed-in views or specific examples related to the formula. One of these smaller grids is connected by a blue arrow to the main diagrams, suggesting a relationship or context.
The diagrams visually demonstrate how NR PDSCH data, DMRS (Demodulation Reference Signal), and punctured REs are distributed across the resource grid under different LTE-NR shift configurations. The presence of red squares (NR RE punctured due to LTE-CRS RE) highlights the interference or co-existence scenarios between LTE and NR.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4366.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[CB006895-A] 5G-L1-DL shall also support the PDSCH RE puncturing with LTE-CRS REs in shared LTE normal subframes with numLteCrsPorts = 2 as explained above in Loki L1 HW. [End CB006895-A]

[RP003187-L] 5G-L1-DL shall also support the PDSCH RE puncturing with LTE-CRS REs in shared LTE normal subframes with numLteCrsPorts = 2 as explained above in Thor. [End RP003187-L]

[CB009273-A] 5G-L1-DL should also support requirements above on ASOE for FR1 FDD DSS(parity from 5GC001586). [End CB009273-A]

---

For FDD, if the carrier bandwidth is an odd number, PDSCH RE puncturing with LTE-CRS REs in shared LTE normal subframes with [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "numLteCrsPorts = 4". The text is slightly blurry and appears to be in a programming or configuration context.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4369.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0].

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating Resource Element (RE) allocation for NR (New Radio) and LTE (Long-Term Evolution) systems, focusing on the concept of a central subcarrier and its relation to ARFCN (Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number). The diagram is divided into three main sections, each representing a different configuration of `reLteNuShift` values (0 or 3, 1 or 4, and 2 or 5). Each section contains a grid representing Resource Blocks (RBs) and symbols within a slot.
A legend on the top left explains the color coding of the REs:
- Red: NR RE punctured due to LTE-CRS RE
- White: RE not used for NR PDSCH
- Green: NR PDSCH data RE
- Yellow: NR FL DMRS RE
- Purple: NR additional DMRS RE
The vertical axis of each grid represents RBs, with a distinction made between RBs above and below the ARFCN frequency. The horizontal axis represents symbols within an NR slot, which is stated to be 14 symbols long. A red bracket labeled "Central RB" with a red arrow points to a specific RB within the central part of the frequency spectrum, indicating its significance.
The top right of the image shows a zoomed-in view of a portion of the grid, highlighting the distribution of different RE types.
The formula `k = 6*(2*dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte)` is displayed at the top of the image, suggesting a relationship between these parameters.
Each of the three main sections presents a different scenario for `reLteNuShift`:
1. **Left Section:** `reLteNuShift = 0 or 3`. This section shows a grid with 14 symbols horizontally and RBs vertically. The distribution of red, white, green, yellow, and purple REs is depicted.
2. **Middle Section:** `reLteNuShift = 1 or 4`. Similar to the left section, this displays a different RE allocation pattern based on the `reLteNuShift` value.
3. **Right Section:** `reLteNuShift = 2 or 5`. This section shows yet another RE allocation pattern corresponding to these `reLteNuShift` values.
The grids in each section are further divided into two halves vertically, suggesting a division of RBs, possibly related to the "RBs: above the ARFCN frequency" and "RBs: below the ARFCN frequency" labels. The central RB is highlighted in the diagram.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4369.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[CB006895-A] 5G-L1-DL shall also support the PDSCH RE puncturing with LTE-CRS REs in shared LTE normal subframes with numLteCrsPorts = 4 as explained above in Loki L1 HW. [End CB006895-A]

[RP003187-L] 5G-L1-DL shall also support the PDSCH RE puncturing with LTE-CRS REs in shared LTE normal subframes with numLteCrsPorts = 4 as explained above in Thor. [End RP003187-L]

[CB009273-A] 5G-L1-DL should also support requirements above on ASOE for FR1 FDD DSS(parity from 5GC001586). [End CB009273-A]

###### 4.3.7.6.1.2.2 PDSCH Resource Elements mapping around LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH

**4.3.7.6.1.2.2.0-1**  (ID: `11437086`)

PDSCH Resource Elements mapping around LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH
If the lteCrsOption IE is set to 4 (LTEFLEXMATCHPBCH) in DlData\_PdschSendReq, 5G-L1-DL shall withdraw the REs occupied by LTE CRS and LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH while NR PDSCH mapping to avoid collision.

The number of REs occupied by LTE CRS and LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH is computed by 5G-L2-PS and is provided to 5G-L1-DL through numLteCrsMappingRes IE in DlData\_PdschSendReq for each PDSCH granted. This case is used for the scenario of scheduling NR PDSCH by DCI\_1\_1 in LTE subframe 0.

NR PDSCH can be configured as following:

 PDSCH mapping type A, PDSCH duration ld=14

~~o~~~~Slot format: (-) (-) Dc Dd Dd Dd Dd Dd Dd Dd Dd Dd Dd Dd~~

o First 2 symbols for LTE PDCCH, 1 symbol for NR PDCCH (3rd symbol in slot), 11 symbols for NR PDSCH.

 DMRS configuration:

o dlDmrsTypeAPos = 3

o dlDmrsAddPos = 0 (only FL DMRS) or 1 (1 additional DMRS). The additional DMRS position is 12.

For FDD, the supported L1 HW:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | numLteCrsPorts = 2 (5G\_L1\_10108\_replaced([11437159](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_Shw_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | numLteCrsPorts = 4 (5G\_L1\_10108\_replaced([11437159](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_Shw_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | numLteCrsPorts = 1 (5G\_L1\_10109\_replaced([11437205](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_ShxvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) |
| Loner | supported | supported | - |
| Loki | **[5GC001745-C]** Supported in ABIO **[End 5GC001745-C]** **[CB007271-C]** Supported in ABIN **[End CB007271-C]** **[CB009273-A]** Supported in ABOE **[End CB009273-A]** | **[5GC001745-C]** Supported in ABIO **[End 5GC001745-C]** **[CB007271-C]** Supported in ABIN **[End CB007271-C]** **[CB009273-A]** Supported in ABOE **[End CB009273-A]** | **[CB008983-B]** Supported in ABIO/ABIN/ASOE **[End CB008983-B]** |
| Thor | **[RP003187-L]** supported **[End RP003187-L]** | **[RP003187-L]** supported **[End RP003187-L]** | - |

For numLteCrsPorts = 2 or 4
Example of PDSCH REs mapping for LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH rate matching and LTE-CRS rate matching:

-  lteCrsOption = 4 (LTEFLEXMATCHPBCH)

-  PDSCH mapping type A, startSymbol = 3, numOfPdschSymbols = 11

-  dlDmrsTypeAPos = 3, dlDmrsLen = 1, dlDmrsAddPos = 1, numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData = 1 or 2

-  numLteCrsPorts = 2 or 4[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a legend and several diagrams illustrating resource element (RE) allocation in a communication system, likely a mix of LTE and NR (New Radio).
The legend defines the color coding for different types of REs:
- Red: LTE RE (PDCCH or CRS)
- Brown: Nr PDSCH RE ratematched due to LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH location
- Dark Gray: Nr PDCCH RE
- Blue: Nr PDSCH (data) RE
- Light Blue: Nr FL DMRS RE
- Green: Nr additional DMRS RE
- Light Green with dark green squares: Nr PDSCH data RE (CDM group w/o data =1) or Empty RE (CDM group w/o data = 2)
The main diagram on the left shows a time-frequency grid representing a single NR slot of 14 symbols. It depicts the allocation of various channels and signals:
- The first two symbols (0 and 1) are occupied by LTE PDCCH.
- Symbol 2 is occupied by NR PDCCH.
- Symbol 3 is occupied by PDSCH DMRS.
- Symbols 4 to 11 show a mix of PDSCH (data) REs, LTE PSS, LTE SSS, and LTE PBCH. Specifically, LTE PSS is in symbol 5, LTE SSS in symbol 6, and LTE PBCH spans from symbol 6 to 10.
- Symbol 11 is occupied by PDSCH DMRS.
- Symbols 12 and 13 are occupied by PDSCH DMRS.
The diagrams on the right illustrate different "cases" based on `numLteCrsPorts`, which likely refers to the number of CRS (Cell Reference Signal) ports in LTE. Each of these diagrams is also a time-frequency grid representing an NR slot of 14 symbols, with the x-axis representing NR-slot symbols (0-13) and the y-axis representing Resource Blocks (RB).
There are three such diagrams:
1. **Case: numLteCrsPorts = 2**: This diagram shows a grid with red REs in the first two symbols (0-1) across all RBs, indicating LTE CRS. Symbol 2 has gray REs (NR PDCCH). Symbol 3 has light blue REs (Nr FL DMRS RE). The rest of the grid (symbols 4-13) is predominantly green (Nr additional DMRS RE) with scattered red REs (LTE RE (PDCCH or CRS)).
2. **Case: numLteCrsPorts = 4**: This diagram is similar to the previous one, but the red REs (LTE CRS) in symbols 0-1 are more numerous, occupying more RBs. The rest of the grid is similar, with gray, light blue, and green REs, and scattered red REs.
3. **Case: numLteCrsPorts = 2 or 4**: This diagram shows a grid where symbols 0-3 are colored similarly to the previous cases (red, gray, light blue). However, from symbol 4 onwards, a large portion of the grid is filled with brown REs, indicating "Nr PDSCH RE ratematched due to LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH location". There are also scattered green and purple REs.
The diagrams are connected to the main diagram by blue arrows, indicating how certain parts of the main diagram correspond to the configurations shown in the smaller diagrams. For instance, the main diagram's PDSCH DMRS in symbol 3 and symbol 11 are highlighted. The "OR" labels suggest alternative configurations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4381.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note, for the RBs not occupied by LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH, only LTE-CRS rate matching is performed (refer figures in 5G\_L1\_2166\_replaced([11436431](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_QFhPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[RP003187-L] 5G-L1-DL shall also support requirements above in Thor for FR1 FDD (DSS) CPRI. [End RP003187-L]

[CB009273-A] 5G-L1-DL should also support functionality above on ASOE for FR1 FDD DSS(parity from 5GC001745). [End CB009273-A]

---

6 m + (nu + reLteNuShift) mod 6 For dlBandwidth &lt;= m &lt; (2 dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte) k = 1 + 6 m + (nu + reLteNuShift) mod 6 where: if numLteCrsPorts = 1, for l = 4 and 11, nu = 3 AND For reLteNuShift) mod 6 where: if numLteCrsPorts = 1, for l = 7, nu = 0 deltaRbNrLte = (dlBandwidth – lteDlBandwidth) l belongs to OFDM symbol range: [0..13] k belongs to subcarrier range: [0..(12 ###### 4.3.7.6.1.2.3 PDSCH Resource Elements mapping around LTE PSS/SSS

**4.3.7.6.1.2.3.0-2**  (ID: `11437256`)

PDSCH Resource Elements mapping around LTE PSS/SSS
The REs which have to be withdrawn for the case (lteCrsOption = 5 "LTEFLEXMATCHPSS" or 6 "LTEFLEXMATCHPSSALT") consist of two parts:

 The REs (OFDM symbol llteflexmatchpss, subcarrier klteflexmatchpss which are occupied by LTE PSS/SSS:

If lteCrsOption IE is set to 5 (LTEFLEXMATCHPSS)

For 5 <=llteflexmatchpss<= 6, 12*((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3) <=klteflexmatchpss< 12*((dlBandwidth>>1) + 4)

If lteCrsOption IE is set to 6 (LTEFLEXMATCHPSSALT)

If (dlBandwidth = 52) or (dlBandwidth = 106)

for 5 <= llteflexmatchpss <= 6, 12*((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3) <= klteflexmatchpss < 12*((dlBandwidth>>1) + 3)

else

for 5 <= llteflexmatchpss <= 6, 12*((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3) <= klteflexmatchpss < 12*((dlBandwidth>>1) + 4)

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Carrier Bandwidth (MHz) | PRBs | OFDM Symbols (llteflexmatchpss) |
| lteCrsOption = 5 (LTEFLEXMATCHPSS) | 20 MHz (FDD) | [50..56] | {5,6} |
| 15 MHz (FDD) | [36..42] |  |  |
| 10 MHz (FDD) | [23..29] |  |  |
| lteCrsOption = 6 (LTEFLEXMATCHPSSALT) | 20 MHz (FDD) | [50..55] | {5,6} |
| 15 MHz (FDD) | [36..42] |  |  |
| 10 MHz (FDD) | [23..28] |  |  |

* The REs (OFDM symbol lltecrs, subcarrier kltecrs) which are occupied by LTE CRS. The LTE-CRS mapping is same as 5G\_L1\_10099\_replaced([11436274](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_PeffvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[RP003187-L] 5G-L1-DL shall also support requirements above in Thor for FR1 FDD (DSS) CPRI. [End RP003187-L]

[CB009273-A] 5G-L1-DL should also support functionality above on ASOE for FR1 FDD DSS(parity from 5GC001745). [End CB009273-A]

11437368:
Case: numLteCrsPorts = 1, 2 or 4
For 15 MHz bandwidth in FDD DSS, there is no difference between cases “lteCrsOption = 5 (LTEFLEXMATCHPSS)” and “lteCrsOption = 6 (LTEFLEXMATCHPSSALT)”.

For 10 MHz and 20 MHz in FDD DSS, the difference between “lteCrsOption = 5 (LTEFLEXMATCHPSS)” and “lteCrsOption = 6 (LTEFLEXMATCHPSSALT)” for PDSCH REs mapping with LTE PSS/SSS rate matching as following:

* if “lteCrsOption = 5 (LTEFLEXMATCHPSS)”, there is one more guard RB between LTE PSS/SSS and NR PDSCH to prevent interference.
* if “lteCrsOption = 6 (LTEFLEXMATCHPSSALT)”, there is one more RB is allocated to NR PDSCH on OFDM symbol {5,6}.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two identical grids, each representing a resource allocation map for a wireless communication system. The grids are organized with rows labeled by MHz and RB (Resource Block) numbers, and columns representing different types of resource elements (REs). A legend on the right side of the image explains the color coding for each type of RE.
The rows are labeled on the left side, indicating "20 MHz: RB 54" through "20 MHz: RB 57" for the left grid, and similarly for the right grid. Correspondingly, "10 MHz: RB 27" through "10 MHz: RB 30" are also labeled. Each grid is further divided into smaller cells, representing individual resource elements. The rows within each RB block are numbered from 0 to 11.
The legend on the right defines the following RE types:
- **Brown:** LTE PSS/SSS REs
- **Red:** LTE CRS REs
- **White:** Other RE not used by NR PDSCH
- **Light Green:** NrPDSCH (data) RE
- **Black:** empty RE (when DMRS CDM group w/o data = 2)
- **Blue:** NrPDSCH RE (when DMRS CDM group w/o data = 1)
- **Dark Purple:** Nr FL DMRS RE
- **Purple:** Nr additional DMRS RE
The grids themselves show a distribution of these RE types across the resource blocks. For instance, in the "20 MHz: RB 57" row, the first few columns show a pattern of brown, red, and black REs, followed by light green REs. The distribution of colors varies across the different RB blocks and within the columns. The two grids appear to be identical, suggesting a mirrored or duplicated representation of the same resource allocation.
The overall image is a visual representation of how different types of signals and data are allocated within a specific frequency and resource block range in a telecommunications system, likely for LTE and NR (New Radio) technologies.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays two identical grids, side-by-side, each representing a spectrum allocation. The grids are composed of numerous small colored rectangles arranged in columns and rows. The left side of each grid has vertical labels indicating "20 MHz: RB 50" through "20 MHz: RB 53" and "10 MHz: RB 23" through "10 MHz: RB 26". The right grid also has a label "ARFCN" positioned vertically between the two grids.
Each grid has 12 rows labeled from 0 to 11 at the top, and 12 columns of colored rectangles. The columns are colored as follows from left to right: blue, black, brown, red, green, and purple. The colors within the grid represent different resource blocks (RBs) and their allocation.
The left grid shows the following pattern of colored rectangles:
- The first column (blue) has rectangles in rows 0 through 11.
- The second column (black) has rectangles in rows 0 through 11.
- The third column (brown) has rectangles in rows 0 through 11.
- The fourth column (red) has rectangles in rows 0 through 11.
- The fifth column (green) has rectangles in rows 0 through 11.
- The sixth column (purple) has rectangles in rows 0 through 11.
However, there are specific patterns of white rectangles within the colored blocks, indicating unallocated or specific resource usage.
- In the "20 MHz: RB 53" row, there is a white rectangle in the brown column, rows 0-1.
- In the "20 MHz: RB 52" row, there is a white rectangle in the brown column, rows 0-1.
- In the "20 MHz: RB 51" row, there is a white rectangle in the brown column, rows 0-1.
- In the "20 MHz: RB 50" row, there is a white rectangle in the brown column, rows 0-1.
The right grid is a mirror image of the left grid, with the same color patterns and white rectangles in the corresponding positions. The "ARFCN" label suggests that these grids are related to frequency channel numbers.
The table representation of the left grid is as follows:

##### 4.3.7.6.1.3 Asymmetric FDD DSS

###### 4.3.7.6.1.3.1 PDSCH RE mapping around LTE CRS

###### 4.3.7.6.1.3.1.1 Algorithm

**4.3.7.6.1.3.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `12539635`)

Algorithm
Description: when IE isLteCrsMappingEnable in DlCell\_SetupReq message ([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)) is set to TRUE and IE lteDlCenterFrequencyScOffset ([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)) != 0, 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall support rate matching around LTE CRS in asymmetric FDD DSS.

Input:

* The following parameters are provided in DlCell\_SetupReq message ([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)):
* lteDlBandwidth: LTE DL bandwidth.
* reLteNuShift: cell-specific frequency shift (LTE-CRS-vshift).
* numLteCrsPorts: the number of LTE CRS ports.
* lteDlCenterFrequencyScOffset: the subcarrier offset between LTE DL center frequency and NR center frequency.
* dlBandwidth: NR DL bandwidth.
* The following parameters are provided in  DlData\_PdschSendReqL1sw message ([11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)).
* lteCrsOption: indicates PDSCH rate matching around LTE CRS or LTE PBCH/PSS/SSS or LTE PSS/SSS.
* PDSCH grant.

Definition:

1. l belongs to OFDM symbol range: [0..13]
2. k belongs to subcarrier range: [0..(12\*dlBandwidth – 1)] starting from CRB 0 (point A)

PDSCH DMRS configuration:

1. dlDmrsAddPos = 0 (only FL DMRS) or 1 (1 additional DMRS). See [11430831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_EfUPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the additional DMRS position.
2. numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData = 1 or 2

Algorithm:

When IE lteCrsOption equals 1 (LTECRSMAPPING), 5G-L1-DL (BB) shall handle the PDSCH rate matching around LTE CRS for each PDSCH grant with the following steps.

1. 5G-L1-DL (BB) calculates the REs (OFDM symbol l, subcarrier k) overlapping between NR PDSCH and LTE-CRS as below.

// Below LTE DC

For deltaRbNrLte <= m < dlBandwidth

k = lteDlCenterFrequencyScOffset + 6\*m + (nu + reLteNuShift) mod 6

// Above LTE DC

For dlBandwidth <= m < 2\*dlBandwidth - deltaRbNrLte

k = lteDlCenterFrequencyScOffset + 1 + 6\*m + (nu + reLteNuShift) mod 6

where:

* deltaRbNrLte = (dlBandwidth – lteDlBandwidth)
* The supported values of nu in different OFDM symbol l are provided in the following table:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | symbol 4 | symbol 7 | symbol 8 | symbol 11 |
| numLteCrsPorts = 1 | 3 | 0 | - | 3 |
| numLteCrsPorts = 2 | {0, 3} | {0, 3} | - | {0, 3} |
| numLteCrsPorts = 4 | {0, 3} | {0, 3} | {0, 3} | {0, 3} |

1. 5G-L1-DL (BB) performs the NR PDSCH rate matching around LTE CRS for each PDSCH grant in asymmetric FDD DSS.

Output:

1. REs mapping for PDSCH rate matching around LTE CRS.

Examples:

1. See [12539754](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_yksfoCDREfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for examples.

Note:

1. For Asymmetric FDD DSS, see [12543806](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qE7PMCGbEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).
2. The LTE center frequency (Dc) does not carry any LTE signal (see 36.211 §6.12).
3. LTE CRS shall not overlap with NR PDSCH DMRS.
4. From E2E pov, for asymmetric FDD DSS, PDSCH rate matching with LTE CTS with two additional DMRS is not supported. (see 5G UP Algorithms [12518663](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_yRZbghkCEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

The supported technology types are shown in table below:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Technology | Loki | Thor (BB) |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | [CB007372-A] Supported [End CB007372-A] | [CB007372-A] Supported [End CB007372-A] |
| FR1-FDD-eCPRI-7-2a | [CB007372-J] Supported [End CB007372-J] | [CB007372-J] Supported [End CB007372-J] |

---

Legend:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a legend with color-coded entries related to LTE and NR (New Radio) signal components. Each entry consists of a colored rectangle followed by a text description.
The entries are as follows:
- Red: LTE RE (PDCCH or CRS)
- Light Blue: LTE RE (PDSCH)
- Grey: Nr PDCCH RE
- Green: Nr PDSCH (data) RE
- Blue: Nr FL DMRS RE
- Purple: Nr additional DMRS RE
- Green: Nr PDSCH data RE (CDM group w/o data =1) or Empty RE (CDM group w/o data = 2)
- Dark Red: CSI-RS for Acquisition (Ca)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-05261524.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Case 1:  PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS for LTE top alignment without additional DMRS or with one additional DMRS.

* NR: 20 MHz (106 PRBs)
* LTE: 10 MHz (50 PRBs)
* numLteCrsPorts ([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)): 1
* lteDlCenterFrequencyScOffset ([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)): 320

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid-like visualization of LTE resource blocks, organized into rows and columns. Each row represents a different LTE configuration or range of PRBs (Physical Resource Blocks), labeled on the left side. The columns are numbered from 0 to 13, likely representing subcarriers or time slots within a resource block.
The visualization is divided into six main sections, each corresponding to a different `reLteNuShift` value, ranging from 0 to 5, indicated at the bottom of each section. Within each section, there are multiple rows representing different PRB ranges:
\* **Top Row:** Labeled "Last PRB of LTE shared area: 104". This section shows a grid with red, green, blue, and purple colored squares scattered within the green background.
\* **Second Row:** Labeled "PRB 80". Similar to the top row, it displays a grid with colored squares.
\* **Third Row:** Labeled "LTE DC (PRB 78, SC 8)" and "PRB 79". This row is split by a dashed purple line, indicating a dual-connectivity scenario. The upper part shows a grid similar to the previous rows, and the lower part shows another grid with colored squares.
\* **Fourth Row:** Labeled "PRB 55". This section also contains a grid with colored squares.
\* **Fifth Row:** Labeled "First PRB of LTE shared area: 54". This section displays a grid with colored squares.
Each grid within these rows is further divided into smaller cells, with some cells containing red, blue, green, or purple squares. The numbers 0 through 13 are displayed along the bottom of each grid, and numbers 0 through 11 are displayed along the left side of each grid.
The colored squares within the grids likely represent the allocation or presence of specific signals or data within the LTE resource blocks. The different `reLteNuShift` values suggest variations in how these resource blocks are configured or utilized.
The text elements in the image are:
\* "Last PRB of LTE shared area: 104"
\* "PRB 80"
\* "LTE DC (PRB 78, SC 8)"
\* "PRB 79"
\* "PRB 55"
\* "First PRB of LTE shared area: 54"
\* "reLteNuShift = 0"
\* "reLteNuShift = 1"
\* "reLteNuShift = 2"
\* "reLteNuShift = 3"
\* "reLteNuShift = 4"
\* "reLteNuShift = 5"[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-06061403.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

Case 2:  PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS for LTE bottom alignment without additional DMRS or with one additional DMRS.

* NR: 20 MHz (106 PRBs)
* LTE: 10 MHz (50 PRBs)
* numLteCrsPorts ([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)): 1
* lteDlCenterFrequencyScOffset ([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)): -320

[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid-like visualization of resource allocation, likely for a cellular network, across different configurations. The visualization is organized into rows and columns. Each row represents a specific set of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) or a combination of PRBs and subcarriers (SC). The columns represent different values of "reLteNuShift," ranging from 0 to 5.
Each cell within the grid can be one of several colors: green, red, blue, purple, or white. The green cells appear to represent allocated resources, while the colored cells (red, blue, purple) likely indicate specific types of resource usage or control information. The white cells are present in a vertical bar on the left side of each grid, possibly representing a reference or index.
The rows are labeled on the left side:
- "Last PRB of LTE shared area: 51"
- "PRB 27"
- "PRB 26 LTE DC (PRB 26, SC 4)"
- "PRB 2"
- "First PRB of LTE shared area: 1"
The columns are labeled at the bottom with "reLteNuShift =" followed by numbers 0 through 5. Each column contains a set of 14 smaller grids, each with 11 rows (labeled 0 to 10 from bottom to top) and 14 columns (labeled 0 to 13 from left to right).
The colored cells within the green areas show patterns that change with the "reLteNuShift" value. For instance, red cells appear in specific positions within the green grids, and their locations vary across the different "reLteNuShift" columns. Blue and purple cells also appear in distinct patterns.
The "LTE DC (PRB 26, SC 4)" row is visually distinct, with a dashed blue line separating two sections of the grid, suggesting a dual-connectivity scenario.
The image is a visual representation of how resources are mapped or scheduled under varying "reLteNuShift" parameters for different PRB ranges.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-06061403.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

Case 3:  PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS for LTE top alignment.

* NR: 20 MHz (106 PRBs)
* LTE: 10 MHz (50 PRBs)
* numLteCrsPorts ([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)): 1
* lteDlCenterFrequencyScOffset ([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)): 320
* CSI-RS for acquisition on symbol 13.

[image-3][image\_desc]The image displays a grid-like visualization of resource allocation for LTE (Long-Term Evolution) communication. It is organized into rows and columns, with each cell representing a resource element. The visualization is repeated across multiple columns, each labeled with a "reLteNuShift" value from 0 to 5.
The rows are categorized by different LTE configurations: "Last PRB of LTE shared area: 104", "PRB 80", "LTE DC (PRB 78, SC 8)", "PRB 79", "PRB 55", and "First PRB of LTE shared area: 54". Each of these categories spans across the columns representing different "reLteNuShift" values.
Within each grid, the columns are numbered from 0 to 13, and the rows are numbered from 0 to 11 (with some variations in the numbering display). Different colored cells within the grid likely represent different types of resource allocations or states. Specifically, red cells appear to indicate allocated resources, while green cells represent available resources. There are also columns of colored cells on the left side of each grid, with colors including red, white, blue, green, purple, and brown. These might represent control regions or specific signaling information.
The "reLteNuShift" parameter likely influences the distribution or pattern of the allocated resources (red cells) within the grid. Observing the patterns, it appears that as "reLteNuShift" increases, the positions of the red cells shift or change in a systematic way across the different LTE configurations. For instance, in the "PRB 80" row, the red cells are distributed in a particular pattern for `reLteNuShift = 0`, and this pattern is altered for `reLteNuShift = 1`, `reLteNuShift = 2`, and so on. The "LTE DC (PRB 78, SC 8)" row shows a distinct horizontal division, suggesting a dual-connectivity scenario where different PRBs (Physical Resource Blocks) are allocated.
The overall image serves to illustrate how resource allocation changes or is configured based on the "reLteNuShift" parameter for various LTE scenarios.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-06101539.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

The supported technology types are shown in table below:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Technology | Loki | Thor (BB) |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | [CB007372-A] Supported [End CB007372-A] | [CB007372-A] Supported [End CB007372-A] |
| FR1-FDD-eCPRI-7-2a | [CB007372-J] Supported [End CB007372-J] | [CB007372-J] Supported [End CB007372-J] |

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a legend with color-coded entries related to LTE and NR (New Radio) signal components. Each entry consists of a colored rectangle followed by a text description.
The entries are as follows:
- Red: LTE RE (PDCCH or CRS)
- Light Blue: LTE RE (PDSCH)
- Grey: Nr PDCCH RE
- Green: Nr PDSCH (data) RE
- Blue: Nr FL DMRS RE
- Purple: Nr additional DMRS RE
- Green: Nr PDSCH data RE (CDM group w/o data =1) or Empty RE (CDM group w/o data = 2)
- Dark Red: CSI-RS for Acquisition (Ca)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-05261524.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Case 1:  PDSCH rate matching around LTE-CRS for LTE top alignment without additional DMRS or with one additional DMRS.

* NR: 35 MHz (188 PRBs)
* LTE: 15 MHz (75 PRBs)
* numLteCrsPorts ([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)): 2
* lteDlCenterFrequencyScOffset ([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)): 660

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid-like representation of LTE resource blocks, illustrating different configurations based on `reLteNuShift` values. The grids are organized into three rows, each representing a different set of PRBs (Physical Resource Blocks). The first row shows the "Last PRB of LTE shared area: 186". The second row, labeled "PRB 149" and "PRB 148", depicts an "LTE DC (PRB 149, SC 0)" configuration, split by a horizontal dashed purple line. The third row shows the "First PRB of LTE shared area: 111".
Each row contains three sub-grids, corresponding to `reLteNuShift` values of "0 or 3", "1 or 4", and "2 or 5" respectively, as indicated at the bottom of the image.
Within each sub-grid, the columns are numbered from 0 to 13, and the rows are numbered from 0 to 11. The cells within the grids are colored to represent different types of resource elements. There are distinct vertical bands of colors on the left side of each sub-grid: red, grey, blue, and purple. The majority of the grid area is green, with scattered red and purple cells.
The arrangement of colored cells, particularly the red and purple ones, varies across the different `reLteNuShift` configurations. The purple dashed line in the second row indicates a division within the LTE DC configuration.
The text elements present in the image are:
- "Last PRB of LTE shared area: 186"
- "PRB 149"
- "LTE DC (PRB 149, SC 0)"
- "PRB 148"
- "First PRB of LTE shared area: 111"
- "reLteNuShift = 0 or 3"
- "reLteNuShift = 1 or 4"
- "reLteNuShift = 2 or 5"
- Numbers 0 through 13 along the x-axis of each sub-grid.
- Numbers 0 through 11 along the y-axis of each sub-grid.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-06061405.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

* LTE: 15 MHz (75 PRBs)

[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid-like representation of Resource Blocks (RBs) in LTE (Long-Term Evolution) communication. The grids are organized into rows and columns, with specific cells colored to indicate different types of resource allocation or states. The image is divided into three main sections, each representing a different scenario or configuration.
The top section shows a single row of three grids, labeled "Last PRB of LTE shared area: 76". Each grid has 14 columns labeled 0 through 13 and 12 rows labeled 0 through 11. Within these grids, there are colored cells: red, blue, green, and purple. The red cells appear in the first two columns of each grid. Blue cells are in the third column, green cells occupy the majority of the grid from column 4 to 11, and purple cells are in columns 12 and 13. Some individual green cells are highlighted in red, indicating occupied resources.
The middle section is divided by a dashed blue line, indicating "LTE DC (PRB 39, SC 0)". Above this line, there is a single grid labeled "PRB 39". Below this line, there is a single grid labeled "PRB 38". Both these grids have the same structure as the grids in the top section, with red, blue, green, and purple columns, and some red-highlighted green cells.
The bottom section shows two rows of grids, each row containing two grids. The top row of this section is labeled "PRB 39" and the bottom row is labeled "PRB 38". Each of these grids also follows the same color and cell-highlighting pattern.
Below each column of grids, there are labels indicating "reLteNuShift = 0 or 3", "reLteNuShift = 1 or 4", and "reLteNuShift = 2 or 5". This suggests that the different configurations of colored cells within the grids are related to variations in the "reLteNuShift" parameter.
The overall image appears to be a visualization of resource allocation patterns in LTE, possibly demonstrating how different parameters affect the distribution of resources within the time-frequency domain. The colors likely represent different types of control or data channels, and the red-highlighted cells indicate the presence of data or specific control information.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-06061406.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

* lteDlCenterFrequencyScOffset ([11512142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRmwvfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)): -660
